+\subsubsection{Kiß╗ām thß╗Ł Mß║Īch Tuß║¦n tß╗▒ (Testing Sequential Circuits)}
+Mß╗Öt trong nhß╗»ng ─æ├│ng g├│p quan trß╗Źng nhß║źt cß╗¦a DFT l├Ā l├Ām cho mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ (sequential circuits) c├│ thß╗ā kiß╗ām thß╗Ł ─æŲ░ß╗Żc. Viß╗ćc tß║Īo mß║½u kiß╗ām thß╗Ł ho├Ān chß╗ēnh cho mß╗Öt mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ v├┤ c├╣ng phß╗®c tß║Īp v├Ā ngay cß║Ż khi c├│, viß╗ćc ├Īp dß╗źng ch├║ng ─æ├▓i hß╗Åi qu├Ī nhiß╗üu chu kß╗│ xung nhß╗ŗp ─æß╗ā mß║Īch ─æi v├Āo c├Īc trß║Īng th├Īi k├Łch hoß║Īt lß╗Śi.
 
-NhŲ░ minh hoß║Ī ß╗¤ H├¼nh \ref{fig:mux_dff}, mß╗Öt \textbf{bß╗Ö chß╗Źn k├¬nh 2:1 (2-to-1 Multiplexer)} ─æŲ░ß╗Żc gß║»n ngay trŲ░ß╗øc ng├Ą v├Āo D cß╗¦a Flip-Flop. Bß╗Ö chß╗Źn k├¬nh n├Āy c├│ ch├ón ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān gß╗Źi l├Ā \texttt{SE} (Scan Enable) hoß║Ęc \texttt{NbarT} (Normal/Test mode). Tuß╗│ v├Āo gi├Ī trß╗ŗ cß╗¦a t├Łn hiß╗ću ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān n├Āy, mß║Īch sß║Į hoß║Īt ─æß╗Öng ß╗¤ mß╗Öt trong hai chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö:
+Kß╗╣ thuß║Łt DFT thay ─æß╗Ģi cß║źu tr├║c cß╗¦a mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ sao cho c├Īc kß╗╣ thuß║Łt kiß╗ām thß╗Ł mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ├Īp dß╗źng l├¬n n├│.  
 
-\begin{itemize}
-    \item \textbf{Chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö hoß║Īt ─æß╗Öng b├¼nh thŲ░ß╗Øng (Normal Mode, SE = 0):} Bß╗Ö chß╗Źn k├¬nh cho ph├®p t├Łn hiß╗ću tß╗½ ─æŲ░ß╗Øng dß╗» liß╗ću logic (Data In) ─æi qua. Flip-Flop hoß║Īt ─æß╗Öng nhŲ░ b├¼nh thŲ░ß╗Øng trong hß╗ć thß╗æng, nhß║Łn dß╗» liß╗ću tß╗½ mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp ph├Ła trŲ░ß╗øc.
-    \item \textbf{Chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö kiß╗ām thß╗Ł (Test/Shift Mode, SE = 1):} Bß╗Ö chß╗Źn k├¬nh \textit{ngß║»t} ─æŲ░ß╗Øng dß╗» liß╗ću logic v├Ā thay v├Āo ─æ├│ cho ph├®p t├Łn hiß╗ću tß╗½ ch├ón \texttt{SI} (Scan Input) ─æi qua. Khi tß║źt cß║Ż c├Īc SFF trong mß║Īch ─æß╗üu ß╗¤ chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö n├Āy, ch├║ng li├¬n kß║┐t vß╗øi nhau th├Ānh mß╗Öt \textbf{thanh ghi dß╗ŗch (Shift Register)} d├Āi. Ta c├│ thß╗ā ``dß╗ŗch'' dß╗» liß╗ću v├Āo (Shift In) qua ch├ón \texttt{SI} v├Ā ``dß╗ŗch'' dß╗» liß╗ću ra (Shift Out) ─æß╗ā quan s├Īt.
-\end{itemize}
+\paragraph{M├┤ h├¼nh Huffman cho Mß║Īch Tuß║¦n tß╗▒}
+NhŲ░ ─æ├Ż tr├¼nh b├Āy, mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ ─æŲ░ß╗Żc m├┤ h├¼nh h├│a bß║▒ng mß╗Öt khß╗æi tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp c├╣ng vß╗øi phß║Żn hß╗ōi th├┤ng qua c├Īc phß║¦n tß╗Ł trß╗ģ (─æß╗æi vß╗øi mß║Īch ─æß╗ōng bß╗Ö, ─æ├│ l├Ā c├Īc flip-flop c├│ xung nhß╗ŗp). ─Éß║¦u v├Āo v├Ā ─æß║¦u ra sŲĪ cß║źp cß╗¦a mß║Īch chß╗ē kß║┐t nß╗æi v├Āo phß║¦n tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp. T├Łn hiß╗ću ─æß╗ōng hß╗ō (clock), set, reset chß╗ē t├Īc ─æß╗Öng v├Āo flip-flop phß║Żn hß╗ōi. M├┤ h├¼nh n├Āy t├Īch biß╗ćt ho├Ān to├Ān c├Īc thanh ghi (registers) v├Ā phß║¦n mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp (buses, khß╗æi logic, to├Īn hß╗Źc, ...).
 
-Nhß╗Ø cŲĪ chß║┐ n├Āy, mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ phß╗®c tß║Īp ─æŲ░ß╗Żc \textbf{``tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp ho├Ī''} (combinationalized) -- tß╗®c l├Ā tß╗½ g├│c nh├¼n kiß╗ām thß╗Ł, c├Īc trß║Īng th├Īi nß╗Öi tß║Īi trŲ░ß╗øc ─æ├óy ``ß║®n'' b├¬n trong giß╗Ø ─æ├óy ─æß╗üu c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc \textbf{─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān} (controllable) v├Ā \textbf{quan s├Īt} (observable) th├┤ng qua ─æŲ░ß╗Øng scan.
+\paragraph{M├┤ h├¼nh Mß║Īch Trß║Żi phß║│ng (Unfolding Sequential Model)}
+M├┤ h├¼nh trß║Żi phß║│ng (Unfolded circuit model) ph├ón t├Īch bß║Īch c├Īc thanh ghi (phß║¦n tuß║¦n tß╗▒) v├Ā phß║¦n tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp. C├Īc ─æß║¦u ra cß╗¦a thanh ghi trß╗¤ th├Ānh "─æß║¦u v├Āo giß║Ż" (pseudo inputs - PPI) cho mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp, v├Ā ─æß║¦u v├Āo cß╗¦a thanh ghi trß╗¤ th├Ānh "─æß║¦u ra giß║Ż" (pseudo outputs - PPO). Do ─æa phß║¦n c├Īc cß╗Ģng logic cß╗¦a mß║Īch nß║▒m ß╗¤ phß║¦n tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp, n├¬n viß╗ćc kiß╗ām thß╗Ł phß║¦n n├Āy v├Ā t├Łnh lu├┤n c├Īc cß╗Ģng v├Āo ra t─®nh cß╗¦a thanh ghi sß║Į bao qu├Īt ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ─æa sß╗æ lß╗Śi cß╗¦a thiß║┐t kß║┐.
 
-\subsection{Quy tr├¼nh kiß╗ām thß╗Ł Shift-Capture-Shift}
-\textbf{[Bß╗Ģ sung kiß║┐n thß╗®c cß╗¦a nh├│m]} To├Ān bß╗Ö quy tr├¼nh kiß╗ām thß╗Ł bß║▒ng Scan tu├ón theo chu tr├¼nh 3 bŲ░ß╗øc lß║Ęp ─æi lß║Ęp lß║Īi cho mß╗Śi test vector:
+Kß║┐t quß║Ż kiß╗ām thß╗Ł tr├¬n m├┤ h├¼nh trß║Żi phß║│ng sß║Į ─æŲ░ß╗Żc sß╗Ł dß╗źng bß╗¤i c├Īc thiß║┐t bß╗ŗ kiß╗ām thß╗Ł tß╗▒ ─æß╗Öng (ATE) ─æß╗ā kiß╗ām tra mß║Īch thß╗▒c tß║┐. ─Éoß║Īn m├Ż Verilog m├┤ phß╗Ång m├┤i trŲ░ß╗Øng kiß╗ām thß╗Ł n├Āy ─æŲ░ß╗Żc gß╗Źi l├Ā mß╗Öt "virtual tester" (thiß║┐t bß╗ŗ kiß╗ām thß╗Ł ß║Żo).
 
-\begin{enumerate}
-    \item \textbf{Shift-In:} Chuyß╗ān mß║Īch sang chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö Test (SE = 1). Dß╗ŗch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ tß╗½ng bit cß╗¦a test vector v├Āo chuß╗Śi scan th├┤ng qua ch├ón SI. Qu├Ī tr├¼nh n├Āy tß╗æn $L$ chu kß╗│ xung nhß╗ŗp (clock cycles), trong ─æ├│ $L$ l├Ā chiß╗üu d├Āi chuß╗Śi scan (sß╗æ lŲ░ß╗Żng SFF).
-    \item \textbf{Capture:} Chuyß╗ān mß║Īch sang chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö Normal (SE = 0) trong ─æ├║ng \textbf{1 chu kß╗│ clock}. L├║c n├Āy, mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp t├Łnh to├Īn dß╗▒a tr├¬n gi├Ī trß╗ŗ vß╗½a ─æŲ░ß╗Żc nß║Īp v├Āo c├Īc Flip-Flop, v├Ā kß║┐t quß║Ż ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ``chß╗æt'' (capture) lß║Īi v├Āo ch├Łnh c├Īc Flip-Flop ─æ├│.
-    \item \textbf{Shift-Out:} Chuyß╗ān lß║Īi sang chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö Test (SE = 1). Dß╗ŗch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ c├Īc gi├Ī trß╗ŗ ─æ├Ż ─æŲ░ß╗Żc capture ra ngo├Āi qua ch├ón SO ─æß╗ā ─æem so s├Īnh vß╗øi kß║┐t quß║Ż mong ─æß╗Żi. ─Éß╗ōng thß╗Øi, trong l├║c shift-out, ta c├│ thß╗ā shift-in test vector tiß║┐p theo, gi├║p tiß║┐t kiß╗ćm thß╗Øi gian.
-\end{enumerate}
+\subsubsection{T├Łnh kiß╗ām thß╗Ł cß╗¦a Mß║Īch Tß╗Ģ Hß╗Żp (Testability of Combinational Circuits)}
+C├Īc mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp c┼®ng hŲ░ß╗¤ng lß╗Żi tß╗½ DFT. Bß║▒ng c├Īch ch├©n th├¬m phß║¦n cß╗®ng, sß╗æ lŲ░ß╗Żng vector kiß╗ām thß╗Ł c├│ thß╗ā giß║Żm xuß╗æng hoß║Ęc ─æß╗Ö phß╗¦ lß╗Śi ─æŲ░ß╗Żc t─āng l├¬n.
 
-% ======================================================================
-% Mß╗żC 2: FULL SCAN TR├ŖN ADDING MACHINE (Dß╗ŗch tß╗½ gi├Īo tr├¼nh)
-% ======================================================================
-\section{Kiß╗ām thß╗Ł Qu├®t To├Ān cß╗źc tr├¬n Adding Machine (7.5.1 Full Scan Adding Machine)}
+\subsection{Ch├©n T├Łnh Kiß╗ām Thß╗Ł (Testability Insertion)}
+Chß╗ē cß║¦n th├¬m mß╗Öt bß╗Ö dß║½n t├Łn hiß╗ću, hay mß╗Öt ch├ón input/output, ta ─æ├Ż biß║┐n mß║Īch trß╗¤ n├¬n dß╗ģ kiß╗ām thß╗Ł hŲĪn. Mß╗źc n├Āy sß║Į ─æß╗ü cß║Łp ─æß║┐n c├Īc lß╗▒a chß╗Źn cß║źu tr├║c phß║¦n cß╗®ng gi├║p kiß╗ām thß╗Ł mß║Īch qua viß╗ćc ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān v├Ā quan s├Īt c├Īc node.
 
-\subsection{Cß║źu h├¼nh mß║Īch sau khi ch├©n Scan}
-Vß╗øi cŲĪ chß║┐ qu├®t (scan) ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ch├©n v├Āo hß╗ć thß╗æng Adding Machine nhŲ░ thß╗ā hiß╗ćn ß╗¤ H├¼nh 7.52 trong s├Īch gß╗æc, ─æŲ░ß╗Øng dß║½n t├Łn hiß╗ću \texttt{data\_bus\_in} trß╗¤ th├Ānh ng├Ą v├Āo ch├Łnh (primary input) cß╗¦a mß║Īch, c├▓n \texttt{data\_bus\_out} c├╣ng vß╗øi \texttt{ad\_bus} hß╗Żp lß║Īi tß║Īo th├Ānh cß╗źm ng├Ą ra ch├Łnh (primary output). Mß║Īch khß║Ż kiß╗ām thu ─æŲ░ß╗Żc c├│ tß╗Ģng cß╗Öng \textbf{24 ng├Ą v├Āo giß║Ż (pseudo primary inputs)} v├Ā \textbf{24 ng├Ą ra giß║Ż (pseudo primary outputs)}, lß║¦n lŲ░ß╗Żt l├Ā c├Īc ng├Ą ra v├Ā ng├Ą v├Āo cß╗¦a c├Īc thanh ghi (registers) nß╗Öi tß║Īi.
+\subsubsection{Cß║Żi thiß╗ćn Khß║Ż n─āng Quan s├Īt (Improving Observability)}
+Cß║Żi thiß╗ćn khß║Ż n─āng quan s├Īt gi├║p hiß╗ću ß╗®ng lß╗Śi dß╗ģ d├Āng truyß╗ün ra ngo├Āi qua c├Īc ch├ón ─æß║¦u ra sŲĪ cß║źp thay v├¼ bß╗ŗ kß║╣t trong mß║Īch. Theo t├Łnh to├Īn quan s├Īt (nhŲ░ SCOAP), c├Īc ─æŲ░ß╗Øng t├Łn hiß╗ću c├│ ─æß╗Ö quan s├Īt thß║źp n├¬n ─æŲ░ß╗Żc nß╗æi ra ch├ón ngo├Āi hoß║Ęc ─æŲ░a qua c├Īc kß╗╣ thuß║Łt nhŲ░ k├®o ─æß║¦u ra flip-flop trß║Īng th├Īi ra thß║│ng ch├ón output ch├Łnh.
+Trong thiß║┐t kß║┐ ß╗¤ mß╗®c RTL, c├Īc ─æŲ░ß╗Øng cß╗Ø ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān tß╗½ controller sang datapath l├Ā nhß╗»ng vß╗ŗ tr├Ł l├Į tŲ░ß╗¤ng ─æß╗ā quan s├Īt. Trß║Īng th├Īi cß╗¦a multiplexer select, register load, count t├Łn hiß╗ću, v.v. c┼®ng rß║źt ph├╣ hß╗Żp.
 
-\subsection{Sinh Test Vector bß║▒ng HOPE}
-─Éß╗ā phß╗źc vß╗ź kiß╗ām thß╗Ł mß║Īch n├Āy, mß╗Öt \textbf{m├┤ h├¼nh trß║Żi phß║│ng (unfolded model)} cß╗¦a to├Ān mß║Īch ─æ├Ż ─æŲ░ß╗Żc tß║Īo ra, ho├Ān to├Ān tŲ░ŲĪng tß╗▒ c├Īch ─æ├Ż thß╗▒c hiß╗ćn cho v├Ł dß╗ź mß║Īch Residue-5 ß╗¤ Mß╗źc 7.3 cß╗¦a s├Īch. Mß║Īng li├¬n kß║┐t tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp (combinational netlist) thu ─æŲ░ß╗Żc sau khi trß║Żi phß║│ng ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ─æŲ░a v├Āo c├┤ng cß╗ź sinh mß║½u kiß╗ām thß╗Ł tß╗▒ ─æß╗Öng (ATPG) c├│ t├¬n l├Ā \textbf{HOPE} ─æß╗ā ph├ón t├Łch cho tß╗Ģng cß╗Öng 976 lß╗Śi (faults).
+\begin{figure}[H]
+    \centering
+    \includegraphics[width=0.7\textwidth]{images/mux_dff.png}
+    \caption{\textit{Kß╗╣ thuß║Łt cß║Żi thiß╗ćn khß║Ż n─āng quan s├Īt v├Ā ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān cŲĪ bß║Żn.}}
+\end{figure}
 
-Kß║┐t quß║Ż m├Ā HOPE trß║Ż vß╗ü:
+\subsubsection{Cß║Żi thiß╗ćn Khß║Ż n─āng ─Éiß╗üu khiß╗ān (Improving Controllability)}
+─Éß╗ā tß║Īo t├Łnh kiß╗ām thß╗Ł cho c├Īc khß╗æi tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp s├óu b├¬n trong, ta c├│ thß╗ā d├╣ng phß║¦n cß╗®ng can thiß╗ćp v├Āo ─æŲ░ß╗Øng t├Łn hiß╗ću giß╗»a 2 khß╗æi logic. C├│ nhiß╗üu kß╗╣ thuß║Łt ch├©n:
 \begin{itemize}
-    \item Tß║Īo ra \textbf{64 test vectors} (bß╗Ö vector kiß╗ām thß╗Ł).
-    \item ─Éß║Īt \textbf{─æß╗Ö phß╗¦ lß╗Śi (fault coverage) = 80.53\%}.
-    \item Mß╗Śi test vector gß╗ōm \textbf{32 bit ng├Ą v├Āo} v├Ā \textbf{38 bit ng├Ą ra}:
-    \begin{itemize}
-        \item Ph├Ła input: 8 bit d├Ānh cho \texttt{data\_bus\_in} (─æŲ░ß╗Øng song song) + 24 bit d├Ānh cho ─æŲ░ß╗Øng scan (─æŲ░ß╗Øng nß╗æi tiß║┐p).
-        \item Ph├Ła output: 8 bit tß╗½ \texttt{data\_bus\_out} + 6 bit tß╗½ \texttt{ad\_bus} + 24 bit tß╗½ ─æŲ░ß╗Øng scan ra.
-    \end{itemize}
+    \item \textbf{0-insertion:} ├ēp mß╗Öt ─æŲ░ß╗Øng d├óy mang gi├Ī trß╗ŗ 0 bß║▒ng cß║źu tr├║c cß╗Ģng logic v├Ā mß╗Öt ch├ón test.
+    \item \textbf{1-insertion:} ├ēp mß╗Öt ─æŲ░ß╗Øng d├óy mang gi├Ī trß╗ŗ 1 bß║▒ng cß║źu tr├║c OR gate.
+    \item \textbf{0 and 1-insertion:} Cho ph├®p ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān ch├©n cß║Ż gi├Ī trß╗ŗ 0 lß║½n 1 th├┤ng qua cß║źu tr├║c phß╗®c hß╗Żp.
+    \item \textbf{Test Data Multiplexing (Ch├©n dß╗» liß╗ću kiß╗ām thß╗Ł):} D├╣ng bß╗Ö dß╗ōn k├¬nh (MUX) ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān bß╗¤i t├Łn hiß╗ću \texttt{NbarT}. Khi $\texttt{NbarT} = 0$, mß║Īch ß╗¤ chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö thŲ░ß╗Øng. Khi $\texttt{NbarT} = 1$, t├Łn hiß╗ću kiß╗ām thß╗Ł tß╗½ input ngo├Āi sß║Į ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ─æŲ░a thß║│ng v├Āo ng├Ą v├Āo cß╗¦a khß╗æi logic cß║¦n kiß╗ām tra.
 \end{itemize}
 
-\subsection{M├┤ h├¼nh Tester ß║Żo (Virtual Tester)}
-M├┤ h├¼nh khß║Ż kiß╗ām ho├Ān chß╗ēnh cß╗¦a Adding Machine thu ─æŲ░ß╗Żc bß║▒ng c├Īch x├óu chuß╗Śi tß║źt cß║Ż c├Īc flip-flop cß╗¦a mß║Īch th├Ānh \textbf{mß╗Öt chuß╗Śi scan duy nhß║źt (single chain)}. H├¼nh \ref{lst:753} tr├¼nh b├Āy bß╗Ö tester ß║Żo (virtual tester) d├╣ng cho viß╗ćc kiß╗ām thß╗Ł full scan tr├¬n mß║Īch khß║Ż kiß╗ām n├Āy. Ngoß║Īi trß╗½ mß║Īch ─æang kiß╗ām thß╗Ł (circuit under test) l├Ā kh├Īc biß╗ćt, v├Ā sß╗æ lß║¦n dß╗ŗch (shifts) trong c├Īc scan flip-flop kh├Īc nhau, \textbf{bß╗Ö tester n├Āy ho├Ān to├Ān tŲ░ŲĪng tß╗▒ vß╗øi bß╗Ö tester cß╗¦a mß║Īch Residue-5} ─æ├Ż ─æŲ░ß╗Żc thß║Żo luß║Łn ß╗¤ Mß╗źc 7.3 cß╗¦a s├Īch. H├¼nh \ref{lst:753} chß╗ē hiß╗ān thß╗ŗ phß║¦n v├▓ng lß║Ęp chß╗ŗu tr├Īch nhiß╗ćm ├Īp dß╗źng c├Īc test vector v├Āo netlist ─æ├Ż ch├©n scan.
+\subsubsection{Chia sß║╗ Ch├ón Quan s├Īt v├Ā Ch├ón ─Éiß╗üu khiß╗ān}
+Viß╗ćc th├¬m qu├Ī nhiß╗üu ch├ón ─æß║¦u ra hoß║Ęc ─æß║¦u v├Āo sŲĪ cß║źp l├Ā kh├┤ng thß╗▒c tß║┐. Do ─æ├│, mß║Īch sß╗Ł dß╗źng Multiplexer ─æß╗ā dß╗ōn nhiß╗üu node quan s├Īt v├Āo mß╗Öt ch├ón output duy nhß║źt (cß║¦n $\log_2 n$ ch├ón chß╗Źn select). TŲ░ŲĪng tß╗▒, mß╗Öt bß╗Ö Demultiplexer c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc d├╣ng ─æß╗ā lu├ón phi├¬n ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān nhiß╗üu ─æŲ░ß╗Øng d├óy kh├Īc nhau (Test point) chß╗ē vß╗øi mß╗Öt cß╗Ģng ─æß║¦u v├Āo, tiß║┐t kiß╗ćm $n$ ch├ón kiß╗ām thß╗Ł.
+
+─Éß╗ā tiß║┐t kiß╗ćm ch├ón select cß╗¦a MUX/DEMUX hŲĪn nß╗»a, mß╗Öt bß╗Ö ─æß║┐m (Counter) c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc t├Łch hß╗Żp trong chip. Chß╗ē cß║¦n cß║źp xung nhß╗ŗp cho Counter, n├│ sß║Į tß╗▒ ─æß║┐m v├▓ng v├Ā chß╗Źn c├Īc node tŲ░ŲĪng ß╗®ng, ─æß╗Ģi lß║źy thß╗Øi gian kiß╗ām thß╗Ł d├Āi hŲĪn nhŲ░ng kh├┤ng tß╗æn diß╗ćn t├Łch ch├ón chip.
+
+\subsubsection{─Éiß╗üu khiß╗ān v├Ā Quan s├Īt ─Éß╗ōng thß╗Øi (Simultaneous Control and Observation)}
+─Éß╗ā ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān c├Īc t├Łn hiß╗ću ─æß╗ōng thß╗Øi vß╗øi sß╗æ ch├ón hß║Īn chß║┐, kß╗╣ thuß║Łt thanh ghi dß╗ŗch (Shift Register) ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ├Īp dß╗źng ─æß╗ā tß║Żi nß╗æi tiß║┐p dß╗» liß╗ću sau ─æ├│ xuß║źt song song v├Āo c├Īc ─æiß╗ām kiß╗ām thß╗Ł. TŲ░ŲĪng tß╗▒ ─æß╗æi vß╗øi thu thß║Łp ─æß║¦u ra: thu song song v├Ā dß╗ŗch nß╗æi tiß║┐p ra ngo├Āi.
+\paragraph{Isolated Serial Scan} kß║┐t hß╗Żp hai thao t├Īc tr├¬n v├Āo chung mß╗Öt thanh ghi dß╗ŗch. Thanh ghi n├Āy c├┤ lß║Łp khß╗Åi luß╗ōng ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān ch├Łnh qua t├Łn hiß╗ću ph├ón v├╣ng \texttt{NbarT}, vß╗½a dß╗ŗch dß╗» liß╗ću kiß╗ām thß╗Ł v├Āo, vß╗½a nhß║Łn phß║Żn hß╗ōi v├Ā dß╗ŗch ra trong suß╗æt mß╗Öt sß╗æ chu kß╗│ kiß╗ām thß╗Ł.  
+
+\begin{lstlisting}[language=Verilog, caption={M├Ż giß║Ż Verilog cho hß╗ć thß╗æng Isolated Serial Scan (Shift-register)}]
+module shiftreg #(parameter size = 4) (
+    input [size-1:0] parin, 
+    input TestClock, SerialTestDataIn, 
+    input [1:0] TestMode,
+    output SerialTestDataOut,
+    output reg [size-1:0] parout
+);
+    always @(posedge TestClock) begin
+        case (TestMode) 
+            0: parout <= parout;          
+            1: parout <= {SerialTestDataIn, parout[size-1:1]}; // Shift mode
+            2: parout <= parin; // Parallel load
+            3: parout <= 0;     // Reset
+        endcase    
+    end 
+    assign SerialTestDataOut = parout[0]; 
+endmodule
+\end{lstlisting}
 
-\subsection{Giß║Żi th├Łch chi tiß║┐t Testbench}
-Testbench n├Āy ─æß╗Źc lß║¦n lŲ░ß╗Żt \textbf{64 test vectors} tß╗½ tß╗ćp \texttt{testFile} v├Ā ├Īp dß╗źng ch├║ng v├Āo mß║Īch full scan ─æß╗ā kiß╗ām tra tß╗½ng lß╗Śi trong sß╗æ 976 lß╗Śi. Vß╗øi mß╗Śi test vector:
-\begin{enumerate}
-    \item \textbf{8 bit} ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ├Īp v├Āo ─æŲ░ß╗Øng song song (\texttt{PI}).
-    \item \textbf{24 bit c├▓n lß║Īi} ─æŲ░ß╗Żc dß╗ŗch v├Āo nß╗æi tiß║┐p (shifted-in serially) qua v├▓ng lß║Ęp \texttt{repeat(24)} -- mß╗Śi lß║¦n lß║Ęp ─æß║®y 1 bit v├Āo ch├ón \texttt{si} v├Ā ─æß╗ōng thß╗Øi r├║t 1 bit tß╗½ ch├ón \texttt{so}.
-    \item Sau khi dß╗ŗch hß║┐t 24 bit, mß║Īch chuyß╗ān vß╗ü chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö Normal (\texttt{NbarT = 0}) trong \textbf{1 xung clock} ─æß╗ā thß╗▒c hiß╗ćn bŲ░ß╗øc Capture.
-    \item Khß╗æi lß╗ćnh \texttt{if} so s├Īnh chuß╗Śi gh├®p nß╗æi (concatenation) giß╗»a 24 bit output thu thß║Łp tß╗½ ─æŲ░ß╗Øng nß╗æi tiß║┐p v├Ā 14 bit output song song vß╗øi 38 bit ─æß║¦u ra mong ─æß╗Żi tß╗½ \texttt{testFile}. Nß║┐u kh├Īc nhau $\Rightarrow$ lß╗Śi ─æ├Ż ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ph├Īt hiß╗ćn (detected).
-\end{enumerate}
 
-\begin{lstlisting}[caption={H├¼nh 7.53: Bß╗Ö tester ß║Żo cho Full Scan Adding Machine (Full scan Adding Machine virtual tester)}, label={lst:753}]
-module Tester;
-. . .. . .
-CPU_ScanInserted FUT(clk, PI, PO, NbarT, Si, So);
-always #200 clk = ~clk;
-initial begin
-    while( !$feof(faultFile)) begin
-        . . .
-        . . .
-        while((!$feof(testFile))&&(!detected))begin
-            status = $fscanf(testFile,"%b\n",line);
-            pre_expected_st = cur_expected_st;
-            expected_PO = line[out_size - 1:0];
-            cur_expected_st = line[st_size + out_size -1 :out_size];
-            PI = line[st_size+out_size+in_size-1:st_size+out_size];
-            NbarT = 1'b1;
-            #delay;
-            index = stIndex;
-            repeat(24) begin
-                si = line[index];
-                @(posedge clk);
-                saved_st[index - stIndex] = so;
-                index = index + 1;
-            end
-            NbarT = 1'b0;
-            @(posedge clk);
-            SampledPO = PO;
-            if({pre_expected_st, expected_PO} != {saved_st, SampledPO})
-            begin
-                numOfDetected = numOfDetected + 1;
-                detected = 1;
-            end
-            #5;
-        end//test
-        . . .
-    end//fault
-    . . .
-end//initial
+\subsection{Kß╗╣ thuß║Łt Full Scan DFT (Full Scan DFT Technique)}
+Vß╗øi kiß║┐n thß╗®c nß╗ün tß╗½ Phß║¦n 7.2, ta tiß║┐n tß╗øi thß║Żo luß║Łn c├Īc ti├¬u chuß║®n thß╗▒c h├Ānh DFT phß╗Ģ biß║┐n nhß║źt, ─æ├│ l├Ā qu├®t to├Ān phß║¦n (Full scan). Kß╗╣ thuß║Łt n├Āy c├│ ├Į tŲ░ß╗¤ng tŲ░ŲĪng tß╗▒ vß╗øi "Isolated serial scan" nhŲ░ng gi├║p giß║Żi quyß║┐t vß║źn ─æß╗ü bß║▒ng c├Īch t├Łch hß╗Żp lu├┤n thanh ghi dß╗ŗch v├Āo trß╗▒c tiß║┐p c├Īc flip-flop trß║Īng th├Īi cß╗¦a mß║Īch (state flip-flops). Viß╗ćc n├Āy vß╗½a giß║Żm tß╗æi ─æa chi ph├Ł phß║¦n cß╗®ng cß╗ōng kß╗ünh, vß╗½a biß║┐n CUT (mß║Īch cß║¦n kiß╗ām thß╗Ł) tr├¬n nß╗ün tß║Żng tuß║¦n tß╗▒ trß╗¤ th├Ānh mß╗Öt mß║Īch ho├Ān to├Ān mang t├Łnh tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp (combinational circuit).
+
+\subsubsection{Ch├©n Full Scan (Full Scan Insertion)}
+\paragraph{Thanh ghi Scan}
+─Éß╗ā ch├©n scan, ta bß║»t ─æß║¦u vß╗øi m├┤ h├¼nh tuß║¦n tß╗▒ cß╗¦a Huffman. Qu├Ī tr├¼nh Full scan biß║┐n c├Īc phß║¦n cß╗®ng cß╗¦a m├┤ h├¼nh thay ─æß╗Ģi. Phß║¦n thanh ghi cß╗¦a m├┤ h├¼nh Huffman l├║c n├Āy bao gß╗ōm cß║Ż chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö dß╗ŗch nß╗æi tiß║┐p t├Łn hiß╗ću ─æß║¦u v├Āo \texttt{STDI} (Serial Test Data In) v├Āo trong thanh ghi dß╗ŗch, v├Ā lu├ón phi├¬n nß║Īp dß╗» liß╗ću song song ß╗¤ chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö b├¼nh thŲ░ß╗Øng.
+Trong chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö kiß╗ām thß╗Ł, \texttt{NbarT} bß║▒ng 1, thanh ghi trß╗¤ th├Ānh thanh ghi dß╗ŗch. ß╗× chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö b├¼nh thŲ░ß╗Øng, \texttt{NbarT} bß║▒ng 0 v├Ā gi├Ī trß╗ŗ trß║Īng th├Īi kß║┐ tiß║┐p (ns) ─æŲ░ß╗Żc nß║Īp v├Āo trß║Īng th├Īi hiß╗ćn tß║Īi (ps).
+
+\begin{lstlisting}[language=Verilog, caption={M├┤ h├¼nh thanh ghi phß║Żn hß╗ōi thß╗Ł nghiß╗ćm kiß╗āu Huffman}]
+module FBRegister #(parameter size = 4) (
+    input [size-1:0] ns, 
+    input STDI, Clock, NbarT, Reset, 
+    output STDO,
+    output reg [size-1:0] ps
+);
+    always @(posedge Clock) begin
+        if (Reset == 1'b0 ) begin
+            if (NbarT == 1'b0) ps <= ns; 
+            else ps <= {STDI, ps[size-1:1]}; // Chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö Shift
+        end
+        else
+            ps <= 0;  
+    end
+    assign STDO = ps[0] ;
 endmodule
 \end{lstlisting}
 
-\subsection{Ph├ón t├Łch n├║t thß║»t thß╗Øi gian test (Bß╗Ģ sung cß╗¦a nh├│m)}
-\textbf{[Bß╗Ģ sung kiß║┐n thß╗®c cß╗¦a nh├│m]} V├▓ng lß║Ęp \texttt{repeat(24)} ch├Łnh l├Ā ``n├║t thß║»t cß╗Ģ chai'' (bottleneck) lß╗øn nhß║źt cß╗¦a Full Scan. Vß╗øi v├Ł dß╗ź Adding Machine chß╗ē c├│ 24 flip-flop, th├¼ thß╗Øi gian dß╗ŗch kh├┤ng ─æ├Īng kß╗ā. NhŲ░ng h├Ży xem x├®t c├┤ng thß╗®c tß╗Ģng qu├Īt cho thß╗Øi gian test:
+\paragraph{Quy tr├¼nh kiß╗ām thß╗Ł}
+Quy tr├¼nh chß╗ē li├¬n quan ─æß║┐n phß║¦n tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp. Cß║źu tr├║c thß╗Ł nghiß╗ćm tß║Īo ─æß║¦u v├Āo giß║Ż (PPI) ß╗¤ ps v├Ā ─æß║¦u ra giß║Ż (PPO) ß╗¤ ns. Vß╗øi chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö thß╗Ł nghiß╗ćm (\texttt{NbarT}=1), dß╗» liß╗ću ─æŲ░ß╗Żc dß╗ŗch dß║¦n v├Āo trong thanh ghi. Sau khi dß╗ŗch xong to├Ān bß╗Ö bit, phß║¦n thß╗® nhß║źt cß╗¦a dß╗» liß╗ću ─æ├Ż sß║Ąn s├Āng tr├¬n ch├ón v├Āo ps. Phß║¦n thß╗® hai cß╗¦a dß╗» liß╗ću kiß╗ām thß╗Ł ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ─æß║Ęt l├¬n c├Īc pin vß║Łt l├Į (PI). Mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp sß║Į truyß╗ün gi├Ī trß╗ŗ qua v├Ā ─æŲ░a ra thiß║┐t bß╗ŗ ph├ón t├Łch lŲ░u trß╗». Sau ─æ├│, ta tß║»t mß║Īch vß╗ü chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö thŲ░ß╗Øng (\texttt{NbarT}=0), v├Ā cß║źp duy nhß║źt 1 nhß╗ŗp ─æß╗ōng hß╗ō, ─æß║®y ns v├Āo thanh ghi song song. BŲ░ß╗øc cuß╗æi c├╣ng, PPO ─æŲ░ß╗Żc dß╗ŗch ra ─æß╗ōng thß╗Øi vß╗øi chu tr├¼nh nß║Īp mß╗øi cß╗¦a Test.
+
+\subsubsection{Cß║źu tr├║c Flip-flop (Flip-flop Structures)}
+ß╗× tr├¬n ta ─æ├Ż m├┤ phß╗Ång mß╗Öt thanh ghi scan vß╗øi NbarT. C├│ nhß╗»ng cß║źu tr├║c gi├║p tiß║┐t kiß╗ćm ─æß╗Ö trß╗ģ hoß║Ęc cß╗Ģng logic hŲĪn tr├¬n thß╗▒c tß║┐.
+\paragraph{Dß╗» liß╗ću kiß╗ām thß╗Ł dß╗ōn k├¬nh (Multiplexed Test Data)}
+Flip-flop k├©m bß╗Ö phß║Łn Multiplexer d├╣ng NbarT ─æß╗ā chß╗Źn lß╗▒a: khi 0 th├¼ thu \texttt{DataIn}, khi 1 th├¼ thu nß╗æi tiß║┐p \texttt{SerialIn}. ─Éß║¦u ra bß╗ŗ ─æß║Żo dß╗ōn th├Ānh thao t├Īc Reset. DŲ░ß╗øi ─æ├óy l├Ā m├Ż Verilog cß╗¦a phß║¦n tß╗Ł cß╗▒c k├¼ phß╗Ģ biß║┐n n├Āy:
+\begin{lstlisting}[language=Verilog, caption={Cß║źu tr├║c Muxed Scan Flip-Flop}]
+module MuxedFF (
+    input NbarT, Reset, DataIn, SerialIn, Clock, 
+    output reg OutFF
+);
+    always @(negedge Clock) begin
+        if (Reset) OutFF <= 1'b0;
+        else OutFF <= ~NbarT ? DataIn : SerialIn;
+    end 
+endmodule
+\end{lstlisting}
+Vß║źn ─æß╗ü vß╗øi thiß║┐t kß║┐ n├Āy l├Ā trß╗ģ logic t─āng l├¬n v├¼ t├Łn hiß╗ću bß╗ŗ ch├©n ├®p qua Multiplexer ngay cß╗Ła ng├Ą D cß╗¦a Flip-flop, khiß║┐n tß╗æc ─æß╗Ö mß║Īch tß╗Ģng thß╗ā bß╗ŗ chß║Łm ─æi ß╗¤ chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö hoß║Īt ─æß╗Öng b├¼nh thŲ░ß╗Øng.
+
+\paragraph{Hß╗ć thß╗æng xung nhß╗ŗp k├®p (Dual Clocking)}
+V├¼ delay cß╗¦a test chß╗ē cß║¦n qua shift-register ngß║»n ngß╗¦i m├Ā kh├┤ng cß║¦n qua mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp phß╗®c tß║Īp, ta c├│ thß╗ā ─æß╗Ģi sß╗Ł dß╗źng mß╗Öt Clock cß╗▒c nhanh ri├¬ng biß╗ćt cho thao t├Īc test data (TestClock). Flip-flop vß╗øi Dual Clock sß╗Ł dß╗źng logic OR ─æß╗ā trß╗Ön \texttt{DataClock} v├Ā \texttt{TestClock}. D├╣ linh hoß║Īt nhŲ░ng mß║Īch n├Āy dß╗ģ bß╗ŗ hiß╗ćn tŲ░ß╗Żng hazard tß║Īi ch├ón D cß╗¦a flip-flop.
+\begin{lstlisting}[language=Verilog, caption={Dual Clock Scan Flip-Flop}]
+module DualClockFF (
+    input DataIn, DataClock, SerialIn, TestClock, 
+    output reg OutFF
+);
+    wire Clock = DataClock | TestClock;
+    always @(negedge Clock) begin
+        if (DataClock) OutFF <= DataIn;
+        else if (TestClock) OutFF <= SerialIn;
+    end
+endmodule
+\end{lstlisting}
 
-$$T_{test} \approx V \times (L + 1) \times T_{clk}$$
+\paragraph{Flip-flop Hai Cß╗Ģng (Two-port Flip-flops)}
+─Éß╗ā kh├┤ng l├Ām trß╗ģ phß║¦n cß╗Ģng D m├Ā vß║½n t├Īch xung nhß╗ŗp, cß║źu tr├║c 2 port vß╗øi LSSD (Level Sensitive Scan Design - thiß║┐t kß║┐ rß║źt nß╗Ģi tiß║┐ng tß╗½ IBM) bao gß╗ōm 2 latch (master/slave). Viß╗ćc ─æß╗Źc song song d├╣ng \texttt{ClockA} v├Ā \texttt{ClockB}, c├▓n chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö kiß╗ām thß╗Ł th├¼ d├╣ng lu├ón phi├¬n \texttt{ClockC} v├Ā \texttt{ClockB}.
 
-Trong ─æ├│: $V$ = sß╗æ lŲ░ß╗Żng test vectors, $L$ = chiß╗üu d├Āi chuß╗Śi scan (sß╗æ flip-flop), $T_{clk}$ = chu kß╗│ xung nhß╗ŗp.
+\subsubsection{Thiß║┐t kß║┐ v├Ā Kiß╗ām thß╗Ł Full Scan (Full Scan Design and Test)}
+To├Ān bß╗Ö qu├Ī tr├¼nh chuß║®n bß╗ŗ cß║źu tr├║c full scan tß╗½ RTL tß╗øi tester ß║Żo bao gß╗ōm 7 bŲ░ß╗øc. Lß║źy v├Ł dß╗ź mß║Īch ─æß║┐m Residue-5 (─æŲ░ß╗Żc nhß║»c ß╗¤ c├Īc chŲ░ŲĪng trŲ░ß╗øc) ─æŲ░ß╗Żc thiß║┐t kß║┐ bß║▒ng m├┤ h├¼nh tuß║¦n tß╗▒:
+\begin{enumerate}
+    \item \textbf{Thiß║┐t kß║┐ v├Ā Gi├Īm ─æß╗ŗnh (Design Validation):} ─Éß║Żm bß║Żo chß╗®c n─āng gß╗æc chß║Īy ho├Ān hß║Żo tr├¬n testbench (testbench migration).
+    \item \textbf{Tß╗Ģng hß╗Żp sŲĪ ─æß╗ō Netlist:} M├Ż Verilog RTL bi├¬n dß╗ŗch th├Ānh mß╗Öt loß║Īt cß╗Ģng AND, OR th├┤ng qua tß╗Ģng hß╗Żp FPGA th├Ānh netlist chuß║®n (vß╗øi output \texttt{out\_0}, \texttt{out\_1}, ...). T├Łn hiß╗ću clock cß║źp cho hß╗ć logic flip-flop DFF.
+    \item \textbf{Trß║Żi phß║│ng (Unfolding):} Cß║»t ─æß╗®t thanh ghi phß║Żn hß╗ōi. ─Éß╗Ģi cß╗▒c nß╗æi mß║Īch ─æß╗ā input cß╗¦a DFF chuyß╗ān th├Ānh Pseudo Primary Output (PPO) v├Ā output cß╗¦a DFF th├Ānh Pseudo Primary Input (PPI). Hß╗ć tuß║¦n tß╗▒ l├║c n├Āy vß╗Ī th├Ānh hß╗ć tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp.
+    \item \textbf{Khß╗¤i chß║Īy Phß║¦n mß╗üm tß║Īo thß╗Ł nghiß╗ćm tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp (Combinational TPG):} Do hß╗ć ─æ├Ż h├│a tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp, chß║Īy Tool ─æß╗ā sinh vectŲĪ test. V├Ł dß╗ź: Tool t├¼m ra 104 lß╗Śi, tß║Īo 26 mß║½u test vector ho├Ān hß║Żo bao phß╗¦ 100\% (Fault coverage = 100\%). Nß║┐u chß║Īy tuß║¦n tß╗▒ vß╗øi ─æß╗Ö kh├│ c┼®, ─æß╗Ö bao phß╗¦ chß╗ē ─æŲ░ß╗Żc 82.4\%.
+    \item \textbf{Ch├©n mß║Īch Scan thß║Łt (Scan Insertion):} Bß║»t ─æß║¦u nß╗æi tiß║┐p ch├ón xuß║źt nß╗æi tiß║┐p (So) sang ng├Ą v├┤ t├Łn hiß╗ću lß╗æi tiß║┐p cß╗¦a tß║¦ng kia bß║▒ng c├Īc \texttt{Si} v├Ā ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān chung bß╗¤i cß╗Ø \texttt{NbarT}.
+    \item \textbf{X├óy dß╗▒ng bß╗Ö kiß╗ām thß╗Ł ß║Żo (Virtual Tester):} Mß╗Öt "Automated test equipment - ATE" th├┤ng qua phß║¦n mß╗üm Testbench ─æß╗Źc tß╗½ m├┤-─æun tß╗ćp dß╗» liß╗ću test (test data file). ─Éß╗ōng thß╗Øi tß╗▒ ch├©n nhß╗»ng lß╗Śi ngß║½u nhi├¬n trong phß║¦n code ─æß╗ā xem chuß╗Śi 26 vectors n├Āy c├│ t├║m gß╗Źn ─æŲ░ß╗Żc mß╗ø lß╗Śi ─æ├│ hay kh├┤ng.
+\end{enumerate}
 
-Trong thiß║┐t kß║┐ chip thŲ░ŲĪng mß║Īi hiß╗ćn ─æß║Īi, mß╗Öt bß╗Ö xß╗Ł l├Į c├│ thß╗ā chß╗®a h├Āng triß╗ću flip-flop. Nß║┐u ├Īp dß╗źng Full Scan dß║Īng chuß╗Śi ─æŲĪn (single chain), giß║Ż sß╗Ł $L = 1.000.000$ Flip-flop, $V = 10.000$ vectors, $T_{clk} = 10 ns$:
+\begin{lstlisting}[language=Verilog, caption={Tr├Łch xuß║źt chŲ░ŲĪng tr├¼nh Virtual Tester ch├©n lß╗Śi kiß╗ām thß╗Ł scan chain}]
+module Tester;
+    res5_ScanInserted FUT(clk, reset, PI, PO, NbarT, si, so); 
+    always #10 clk = ~clk;
+    initial begin 
+        while( !$feof(faultFile)) begin
+            status = $fscanf (faultFile,"%s s@%b\n",wireName, stuckAtVal);
+            $InjectFault (wireName, stuckAtVal); // Giß║Ż lß║Łp lß╗Śi
+            global_reset = 1'b1; reset = 1'b1; #1;
+            global_reset = 1'b0; reset = 1'b0; 
+            while((!$feof(testFile))&&(!detected) ) begin
+                // Tß║Żi vector, tiß║┐n tr├¼nh ─æß╗Źc v├Ā k├Łch hoß║Īt test
+            end
+            if(detected == 0) $display ("NOT DETECTED = %s s@%b", wireName, stuckAtVal);
+            $RemoveFault(wireName); // Phß╗źc hß╗ōi sau kiß╗ām tra
+        end
+        $stop;
+    end
+endmodule
+\end{lstlisting}
 
-$$T_{test} = 10.000 \times 1.000.001 \times 10 \text{ ns} = 100.000 \text{ gi├óy} \approx 27.8 \text{ giß╗Ø}$$
 
-Con sß╗æ n├Āy l├Ā kh├┤ng thß╗ā chß║źp nhß║Łn ─æŲ░ß╗Żc trong sß║Żn xuß║źt h├Āng loß║Īt, nŲĪi mß╗Śi gi├óy tr├¬n m├Īy ATE (Automatic Test Equipment) ─æß╗üu tß╗æn chi ph├Ł rß║źt lß╗øn. ─É├óy ch├Łnh l├Ā l├Į do th├║c ─æß║®y sß╗▒ ra ─æß╗Øi cß╗¦a kß╗╣ thuß║Łt \textbf{Multiple Parallel Scans} ß╗¤ mß╗źc tiß║┐p theo.
+\subsection{Qu├®t ─æa luß╗ōng (Multiple Scan)}
+Mß║Ęc d├╣ kß╗╣ thuß║Łt qu├®t chuß╗Śi ─æŲĪn (Single Scan Chain) gi├║p to├Ān bß╗Ö chip trß╗¤ th├Ānh tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp v├Ā sinh mß║½u kiß╗ām tra tß╗▒ ─æß╗Öng dß╗ģ d├Āng vß╗øi ─æß╗Ö phß╗¦ ─æß║Īt 100\%, nhŲ░ng n├│ gß║Ęp hß║Īn chß║┐ ─æ├Īng kß╗ā ß╗¤ thß╗Øi gian kiß╗ām thß╗Ł do viß╗ćc nß║Īp/xuß║źt nß╗æi tiß║┐p (Shift-in/Shift-out) diß╗ģn ra rß║źt l├óu qua mß╗Öt chuß╗Śi qu├®t d├Āi.
 
-% ======================================================================
-% Mß╗żC 3: MULTIPLE SCAN TR├ŖN ADDING MACHINE (Dß╗ŗch tß╗½ gi├Īo tr├¼nh)
-% ======================================================================
-\section{Thiß║┐t kß║┐ Qu├®t ─Éa luß╗ōng cß║źp RTL (7.5.2 RTL Design Multiple Scan)}
+Thß╗Øi gian cho mß╗Öt lß║¦n kiß╗ām thß╗Ł vß╗øi sß╗æ lŲ░ß╗Żng flip-flop l├Ā $N_{FF}$ c├│ chu kß╗│ l├Ā $N_{FF} + 1$. Tß╗Ģng thß╗Øi gian kiß╗ām tra cho $N_{Test}$ vector sß║Į l├Ā:
+\[ T_{test} = N_{Test} \times (N_{FF} + 1) + N_{FF} \]
+Sß╗▒ trß║Ż gi├Ī qu├Ī lß╗øn cho mß╗Öt chip vß╗øi h├Āng triß╗ću b├│ng b├Īn dß║½n dß║½n ─æß║┐n nguy├¬n l├Į **─æa chuß╗Śi qu├®t song song (Multiple Scans)** ra ─æß╗Øi. ├Ø tŲ░ß╗¤ng cŲĪ bß║Żn l├Ā thay v├¼ mß╗Öt ─æŲ░ß╗Øng hß║¦m ─æß╗Öc ─æß║Īo ─æi qua mß╗Źi thanh ghi, hß╗ć thß╗æng sß║Į t├Īch chuß╗Śi d├Āi ra th├Ānh th├Ānh $k$ nh├Īnh nhß╗Å chß║Īy song song vß╗øi nhau. 
 
-\subsection{├Ø tŲ░ß╗¤ng ph├ón chia chuß╗Śi}
-Mß╗źc n├Āy tr├¼nh b├Āy c├Īch triß╗ān khai thiß║┐t kß║┐ qu├®t ─æa luß╗ōng (multiple scan design) tr├¬n mß║Īch Adding Machine ─æ├Ż giß╗øi thiß╗ću ß╗¤ ChŲ░ŲĪng 2 cß╗¦a s├Īch gß╗æc. TŲ░ŲĪng tß╗▒ phß║¦n thß║Żo luß║Łn vß╗ü Full Scan ß╗¤ mß╗źc trŲ░ß╗øc, mß║Ęc d├╣ sŲĪ ─æß╗ō mß║Īch tr├┤ng kh├Īc so vß╗øi m├┤ h├¼nh Huffman ß╗¤ H├¼nh 7.44, nhŲ░ng bß║Żn chß║źt cß║źu tr├║c mß║Īch vß║½n tu├ón theo m├┤ h├¼nh ─æ├│, v├Ā phŲ░ŲĪng ph├Īp qu├®t ─æa luß╗ōng (multiple scan) ß╗¤ Mß╗źc 7.4 ho├Ān to├Ān ├Īp dß╗źng ─æŲ░ß╗Żc cho mß║Īch n├Āy.
+Do ─æ├│, mß╗Śi nh├Īnh sß║Į c├│ ─æß╗Ö d├Āi $N'_{FF} = \lceil N_{FF} / k \rceil$. Thß╗Øi gian kiß╗ām tra nay giß║Żm ─æi $k$ lß║¦n, chß╗ē c├▓n:
+\[ T_{test\_multiple} = N_{Test} \times (N'_{FF} + 1) + N'_{FF} \]
 
-\subsection{Thiß║┐t kß║┐ Qu├®t ß╗¤ Mß╗®c RT (7.5 RT Level Scan Design)}
-Ch├║ng ta sß╗Ł dß╗źng \textbf{3 chuß╗Śi scan c├│ chiß╗üu d├Āi bß║▒ng nhau}:
+\subsubsection{Mß║Īng Cß║źp sß╗æ cß╗Öng (Full Scan on Adding Machine)}
+Chi ph├Ł cho tß╗æc ─æß╗Ö qu├®t trong cŲĪ chß║┐ ─æa luß╗ōng l├Ā sß╗æ lŲ░ß╗Żng ch├ón xuß║źt nhß║Łp sß║Į t─āng l├¬n khi mß╗Śi nh├Īnh qu├®t ─æß╗üu cß║¦n nhß╗»ng pin nß╗æi tiß║┐p ─æß║Ęc th├╣ l├Ā (Serial In v├Ā Serial Out ri├¬ng). V├Ł dß╗ź vß╗øi vi xß╗Ł l├Į nhß╗Å (CPU Adding Machine), chia ra 3 luß╗ōng ri├¬ng rß║Į:
 \begin{enumerate}
-    \item \textbf{Chuß╗Śi 1:} Bao phß╗¦ thanh ghi t├Łch luß╗╣ \texttt{AC} (Accumulator).
-    \item \textbf{Chuß╗Śi 2:} Bao phß╗¦ thanh ghi lß╗ćnh \texttt{IR} (Instruction Register).
-    \item \textbf{Chuß╗Śi 3:} Bao phß╗¦ thanh ghi bß╗Ö ─æß║┐m chŲ░ŲĪng tr├¼nh \texttt{PC} (Program Counter) cß╗Öng th├¬m 2 flip-flop ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān.
+    \item \textbf{Chuß╗Śi 1: Instruction Register (IR):} \texttt{ir\_Si} $\rightarrow$ \texttt{ir\_So} (8 Flip-flops)
+    \item \textbf{Chuß╗Śi 2: Accumulator (AC):} \texttt{ac\_Si} $\rightarrow$ \texttt{ac\_So} (8 Flip-flops)
+    \item \textbf{Chuß╗Śi 3: Program Counter (PC):} \texttt{pc\_Si} $\rightarrow$ \texttt{cntrl\_So} (8 Flip-flops)
 \end{enumerate}
 
-V├¼ k├Łch thŲ░ß╗øc cß║Ż 3 chuß╗Śi scan bß║▒ng nhau, ch├║ng ta c├│ thß╗ā ├Īp dß╗źng cß║źu h├¼nh \textbf{qu├®t song song ─æa chuß╗Śi (multiple parallel scan chains)}, gi├║p ─æŲĪn giß║Żn ho├Ī ─æ├Īng kß╗ā logic ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān scan. H├¼nh \ref{fig:scan_chain} minh hoß║Ī 3 chuß╗Śi scan n├Āy trong sŲĪ ─æß╗ō RTL cß╗¦a Adding Machine.
-

-\subsection{Ch├©n chuß╗Śi Scan v├Āo Netlist}
-C├Īc chuß╗Śi scan ─æŲ░ß╗Żc \textbf{ch├©n thß╗¦ c├┤ng (manually inserted)} v├Āo netlist. Netlist n├Āy ch├Łnh l├Ā kß║┐t quß║Ż cß╗¦a qu├Ī tr├¼nh tß╗Ģng hß╗Żp (synthesize) m├Ż Verilog cß╗¦a Adding Machine. Viß╗ćc ch├©n scan sß║Į g├óy ra thay ─æß╗Ģi ß╗¤ \textbf{hai chß╗Ś}:
-\begin{enumerate}
-    \item \textbf{Cß╗Ģng giao tiß║┐p (ports):} Th├¬m c├Īc ch├ón v├Āo/ra mß╗øi cho ─æŲ░ß╗Øng scan.
-    \item \textbf{Sß║»p xß║┐p flip-flop:} C├Īc flip-flop ─æŲ░ß╗Żc kß║┐t nß╗æi lß║Īi th├Ānh c├Īc chuß╗Śi theo thß╗® tß╗▒ mong muß╗æn.
-\end{enumerate}
-
-H├¼nh \ref{lst:755} hiß╗ān thß╗ŗ mß╗Öt phß║¦n m├Ż nguß╗ōn cß╗¦a netlist ─æ├Ż ─æŲ░ß╗Żc sß╗Ła ─æß╗Ģi. Cß╗ź thß╗ā:
-\begin{itemize}
-    \item \textbf{Ng├Ą v├Āo mß╗øi:} Ch├ón \texttt{NbarT} (chuyß╗ān chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö Normal/Test) v├Ā 3 ch├ón serial input: \texttt{ir\_Si}, \texttt{ac\_Si}, \texttt{pc\_Si}.
-    \item \textbf{Ng├Ą ra mß╗øi:} 3 ch├ón serial scan output: \texttt{ir\_So}, \texttt{ac\_So}, \texttt{cntrl\_So}.
-    \item \textbf{3 khu vß╗▒c ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ─æ├Īnh dß║źu bß║▒ng dß║źu //} trong m├Ż nguß╗ōn tŲ░ŲĪng ß╗®ng vß╗øi 3 chuß╗Śi scan:
-    \begin{itemize}
-        \item Chuß╗Śi 1: Bß║»t ─æß║¦u tß╗½ \texttt{ir\_Si}, ─æi qua \texttt{INS\_1} ─æß║┐n \texttt{INS\_8}, kß║┐t th├║c tß║Īi \texttt{wire\_26} $\rightarrow$ g├Īn cho \texttt{ir\_So}.
-        \item Chuß╗Śi 2: Bß║»t ─æß║¦u tß╗½ \texttt{ac\_Si}, ─æi qua \texttt{INS\_9} ─æß║┐n \texttt{INS\_16}, kß║┐t th├║c tß║Īi \texttt{wire\_52} $\rightarrow$ g├Īn cho \texttt{ac\_So}.
-        \item Chuß╗Śi 3: Bß║»t ─æß║¦u tß╗½ \texttt{pc\_Si}, ─æi qua \texttt{INS\_17} ─æß║┐n \texttt{INS\_24}, kß║┐t th├║c tß║Īi \texttt{wire\_4} $\rightarrow$ g├Īn cho \texttt{cntrl\_So}.
-    \end{itemize}
-    \item \textbf{Tß║źt cß║Ż} c├Īc flip-flop ─æß╗üu sß╗Ł dß╗źng \texttt{NbarT} l├Ām t├Łn hiß╗ću ─æß║¦u v├Āo ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö.
-\end{itemize}
-

+Viß╗ćc sß╗Ł dß╗źng 3 cß╗źm qu├®t song song y├¬u cß║¦u 3 ch├ón lß║źy t├Łn hiß╗ću v├Āo nß╗æi tiß║┐p (Si), 3 ch├ón xuß║źt (So), v├Ā mß╗Öt ch├ón \texttt{NbarT} ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö dung chung.
 
-\subsection{ChŲ░ŲĪng tr├¼nh Test cho Multiple Scan}
-─Éß╗ā viß║┐t chŲ░ŲĪng tr├¼nh test cho thiß║┐t kß║┐ n├Āy, ta cß║¦n lŲ░u ├Į rß║▒ng thiß║┐t kß║┐ c├│ \textbf{3 chuß╗Śi scan c├│ c├╣ng chiß╗üu d├Āi}, sß╗Ł dß╗źng \textbf{chung mß╗Öt xung nhß╗ŗp (clock)}, v├Ā \textbf{chung mß╗Öt t├Łn hiß╗ću ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö} \texttt{NbarT}.
+\subsubsection{Tr├¼nh kiß╗ām thß╗Ł ß║Żo cho luß╗ōng ─æa qu├®t (Virtual Tester for Multiple Scans)}
+Trong m├┤i trŲ░ß╗Øng m├┤ phß╗Ång Verilog, Virtual Tester c┼®ng phß║Żi cß║Łp nhß║Łt cho ph├╣ hß╗Żp. Thay v├¼ thu 24 bits serial cho mß╗Öt mß║Īch theo cß╗źm duy nhß║źt, Tester phß║Żi t├Īch mß║Żng data test ra 3 ch├╣m (mß╗Śi ch├╣m 8 bits) v├Ā ─æß║®y v├Āo \texttt{ir\_Si}, \texttt{ac\_Si}, \texttt{pc\_Si} song song tr├¬n tß╗½ng clock cß║Īnh nhß╗ŗp. TŲ░ŲĪng tß╗▒, bß╗Ö thu nhß║Łn mß║½u tß╗½ 3 cß╗Ģng xuß║źt (\texttt{ir\_So}, \texttt{ac\_So}, \texttt{cntrl\_So}) cß║¦n ph├ón t├Łch dß╗» liß╗ću gß╗Öp mß║Żng theo ─æ├║ng thß╗® tß╗▒ rß╗ōi ─æem so s├Īnh vß╗øi kß╗│ vß╗Źng.
+Khß╗æi lß╗ćnh nß║Īp dß╗» liß╗ću c┼®ng giß║Żm sß╗æ lß║¦n lß║Ęp ─æi tß╗½ 24 chu k├¼ xuß╗æng 8 chu k├¼.
 
-H├¼nh \ref{lst:756} tr├¼nh b├Āy bß╗Ö tester ß║Żo (virtual tester) m├┤ phß╗Ång h├Ānh vi cß╗¦a mß╗Öt thiß║┐t bß╗ŗ ATE v├Ā chŲ░ŲĪng tr├¼nh test tŲ░ŲĪng ß╗®ng. H├¼nh n├Āy chß╗ē hiß╗ān thß╗ŗ phß║¦n khß╗¤i tß║Īo (instantiation) mß║Īch 3 chuß╗Śi scan v├Ā thß╗¦ tß╗źc ├Īp dß╗źng dß╗» liß╗ću test. C├Īc phß║¦n kh├Īc cß╗¦a testbench vß╗ü cŲĪ bß║Żn giß╗æng hß╗ćt vß╗øi m├Ż ─æ├Ż thß║Żo luß║Łn ß╗¤ Mß╗źc 7.3, n├¬n kh├┤ng lß║Ęp lß║Īi ß╗¤ ─æ├óy.
+\subsection{Thiß║┐t kß║┐ Scan g├│c ─æß╗Ö RTL (RT Level Scan Design)}
+Thiß║┐t kß║┐ kiß║┐n tr├║c qu├®t bß║▒ng phŲ░ŲĪng ph├Īp qu├®t to├Ān phß║¦n sau sŲĪ ─æß╗ō tß╗Ģng hß╗Żp cß╗Ģng logic (Gate-level Full Scan) l├Ā c├Īch tiß║┐p cß║Łn ─æi tß╗½ dŲ░ß╗øi l├¬n (Bottom-up). NhŲ░ß╗Żc ─æiß╗ām l├Ā ─æ├┤i khi Tool kh├┤ng hiß╗āu ─æŲ░ß╗Żc vai tr├▓ cß╗¦a tß╗½ng block mß║Īch, dß║½n ─æß║┐n ch├©n m├╣ qu├Īng v├Ā cß╗ōng kß╗ünh. G├│c tiß║┐p cß║Łn qu├®t thiß║┐t kß║┐ ngay tß╗½ tß║¦ng chuyß╗ān giao thanh ghi (Registers-Transfer Level - RTL) sß║Į ─æem lß║Īi tß╗æi Ų░u h├│a cß║źp ─æß╗Ö kiß║┐n tr├║c.
 
-NhŲ░ thß╗ā hiß╗ćn trong m├Ż, khß╗æi instantiation cß╗¦a netlist (m├Ż mß╗Öt phß║¦n hiß╗ān thß╗ŗ ß╗¤ H├¼nh \ref{lst:755}) bao gß╗ōm \texttt{NbarT} c├╣ng c├Īc ng├Ą v├Āo v├Ā ng├Ą ra nß╗æi tiß║┐p cß╗¦a cß║Ż 3 chuß╗Śi scan.
+\paragraph{Qu├®t mß╗®c bß╗Ö phß║Łn (Partial Scan):}
+ß╗× quy m├┤ RTL, lß║Łp tr├¼nh vi├¬n c├│ thß╗ā chß╗ē ─æß╗ŗnh phß║¦n khß╗æi chß╗®c n─āng n├Āo cß║¦n c├│ scan chain (c├│ thß╗ā l├Ā Data Path) v├Ā bß╗Å qua bß╗Ö ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān (Control Path). Viß╗ćc lß╗▒a chß╗Źn dß╗▒a tr├¬n ph├ón t├Łch cß║źu tr├║c tß╗Ģng tß║Żi RTL sß║Į tiß║┐t kiß╗ćm logic so vß╗øi qu├®t 100\%. 
 
-\subsection{Giß║Żi th├Łch chi tiß║┐t quy tr├¼nh Test}
-Thß╗¦ tß╗źc test ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ─æß║Ęt b├¬n trong khß╗æi \texttt{initial} cß╗¦a H├¼nh \ref{lst:756}. Tß║źt cß║Ż c├Īc phß║¦n ─æß╗üu giß╗æng hß╗ćt H├¼nh 7.53 (\ref{lst:753}), \textbf{ngoß║Īi trß╗½ v├▓ng lß║Ęp \texttt{repeat}}. V├▓ng lß║Ęp \texttt{repeat} ß╗¤ ─æ├óy hoß║Īt ─æß╗Öng nhŲ░ sau:
+\paragraph{Qu├®t Cß╗źc bß╗Ö ─Éß╗Öc lß║Łp (Multiple Independent Scans):}
+C├Īc m├┤-─æun ri├¬ng lß║╗ (Datapath, Controller, Register File) ─æŲ░ß╗Żc li├¬n kß║┐t th├Ānh c├Īc chuß╗Śi v├▓ng ─æß╗Öc lß║Łp (isolated scans). Khi kiß╗ām thß╗Ł Datapath, ta c├│ thß╗ā t├Īi sß╗Ł dß╗źng Controller hoß║Ęc thanh ghi kh├┤ng ß╗¤ trß║Īng th├Īi scan ─æß╗ā cung cß║źp dß╗» liß╗ću ß╗Ģn ─æß╗ŗnh, tr├Īnh xung ─æß╗Öt vß╗ü t├Āi nguy├¬n. 
 
-\begin{enumerate}
-    \item Lß║źy tß╗½ng nh├│m \textbf{8 bit} tß╗½ biß║┐n \texttt{line} (chß╗®a test vector ─æß║¦u v├Āo) v├Ā g├Īn ─æß╗ōng thß╗Øi cho 3 ch├ón scan input: \texttt{ir\_Si}, \texttt{ac\_Si}, \texttt{pc\_Si}.
-    \item Viß╗ćc g├Īn n├Āy diß╗ģn ra khi \texttt{NbarT = 1} cho cß║Ż 3 chuß╗Śi (tß╗®c l├Ā ß╗¤ chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö Shift).
-    \item Sau mß╗Śi lß║¦n g├Īn, c├Īc thanh ghi (registers) ─æŲ░ß╗Żc cß║źp xung clock \textbf{─æß╗ōng thß╗Øi (simultaneously)}.
-    \item Sau xung clock, kß║┐t quß║Ż ─æß║¦u ra tß╗½ \texttt{ir\_So}, \texttt{ac\_So}, \texttt{cntrl\_So} ─æŲ░ß╗Żc thu thß║Łp v├Ā lŲ░u v├Āo mß║Żng \texttt{saved\_st}.
-    \item C├Īc gi├Ī trß╗ŗ output ─æŲ░ß╗Żc \textbf{xß║┐p c├Īch nhau 8 bit} trong mß║Żng \texttt{saved\_st} ─æß╗ā ─æß║Żm bß║Żo c├Īc bit ─æß║¦u ra cß╗¦a c├╣ng mß╗Öt chuß╗Śi scan nß║▒m liß╗ün kß╗ü nhau.
-\end{enumerate}
+\subsection{T├│m lŲ░ß╗Żc (Summary)}
+C├Īc kß╗╣ thuß║Łt DFT chuyß╗ān ─æß╗Ģi mß║Īch tß╗½ cß║źu tr├║c hß╗Öp ─æen hoß║Ęc m├┤ h├¼nh trß║Īng th├Īi (State Machine, Huffman Model) phß╗®c tß║Īp th├Ānh luß╗ōng mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp thuß║¦n t├║y ─æß╗ā thß╗▒c thi viß╗ćc sinh mß║½u kiß╗ām tra (ATP Generation). 
 
-\begin{lstlisting}[caption={H├¼nh 7.56: ChŲ░ŲĪng tr├¼nh test cho qu├®t ─æa luß╗ōng song song (Test program for multiple parallel scans)}, label={lst:756}]
-module Tester;
-. . .

-CPU_M_ScanInserted FUT(clk, PI, PO, NbarT, ir_Si, ac_Si, pc_Si, ir_So,
-ac_So, cntrl_So);
-always #200 clk = ~clk;
-initial begin
-    while( !$feof(faultFile)) begin
-        while((!$feof(testFile))&&(!detected)) begin
-            status = $fscanf(testFile,"%b\n",line);
-            pre_expected_st = cur_expected_st;
-            expected_PO = line[out_size - 1:0];
-            cur_expected_st = line[st_size + out_size -1 :out_size];
-            PI = line[st_size+out_size+in_size-1:st_size+out_size];
-            NbarT = 1'b1;
-            #delay;
-            index = stIndex;
-            repeat(8) begin
-                ir_Si = line[index];
-                ac_Si = line[index+8];
-                pc_Si = line[index+16];
-                @(posedge clk);
-                saved_st[index - stIndex] = ir_So;
-                saved_st[index+8 - stIndex] = ac_So;
-                saved_st[index+16 - stIndex] = cntrl_So;
-                index = index + 1;
-            end
-            NbarT = 1'b0;
-            @(posedge clk);
-            SampledPO = PO;
-            if({pre_expected_st, expected_PO} != {saved_st, SampledPO})
-            begin
-                numOfDetected = numOfDetected + 1;
-                detected = 1;
-            end
-            #5;
-        end//test
-        . . .
-    end//fault
-    . . .
-end//initial
-endmodule
-\end{lstlisting}
+Trong thß╗▒c tß║┐, quy tr├¼nh ch├©n qu├®t scan ─æŲ░ß╗Żc vß║Łn h├Ānh theo c├Īc bŲ░ß╗øc: 
+1. Chß║Īy sŲĪ ─æß╗ō chß╗®c n─āng (RTL);
+2. Nhanh ch├│ng ─æŲ░a chuß╗Śi (Partial hoß║Ęc Full Scan) qua c├┤ng cß╗ź tß╗Ģng hß╗Żp;
+3. Cß║»t g├Ży v├▓ng phß║Żn hß╗ōi (Break Feedback Loop) th├┤ng qua Unfolding;
+4. Nß║Īp tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp MUX-DFF (D├╣ng NbarT chuyß╗ān chß║┐ ─æß╗Ö);
+5. Tß║Īo Testbench hoß║Ęc bß╗Ö kiß╗ām thß╗Ł Virtual Tester bß║▒ng Verilog;
+6. N├®n v├Ā chß║Īy tß╗æi giß║Żn chuß╗Śi Test Vector th├┤ng qua c├Īc k─® thuß║Łt Test Compaction ti├¬n tiß║┐n nhß║źt. Tß╗æi Ų░u thß╗Øi gian l├Ā b├Āi to├Īn chi ph├Ł (Tradeoff) giß╗»a sß╗æ ch├ón I/O vß║Łt l├Į tr├¬n bo mß║Īch (Package pins) v├Ā sß╗æ Clock Cycle thß╗▒c tß║┐ mß╗Śi khi k├Łch hoß║Īt kiß╗ām duyß╗ćt chip.
 
-\subsection{So s├Īnh hiß╗ću quß║Ż Full Scan vs Multiple Scan (Bß╗Ģ sung cß╗¦a nh├│m)}
-\textbf{[Bß╗Ģ sung kiß║┐n thß╗®c cß╗¦a nh├│m]} Bß║Żng dŲ░ß╗øi ─æ├óy tß╗Ģng hß╗Żp sß╗▒ kh├Īc biß╗ćt then chß╗æt giß╗»a hai phŲ░ŲĪng ph├Īp khi ├Īp dß╗źng cho mß║Īch Adding Machine (24 flip-flop, 64 test vectors):
-
-\begin{table}[!ht]
-\centering
-\caption{So s├Īnh Full Scan v├Ā Multiple Scan tr├¬n Adding Machine}
-\label{tab:compare}
-\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|}
-\hline
-\textbf{Ti├¬u ch├Ł} & \textbf{Full Scan (1 chain)} & \textbf{Multiple Scan (3 chains)} \\
-\hline
-Sß╗æ chuß╗Śi scan & 1 & 3 \\
-\hline
-Chiß╗üu d├Āi mß╗Śi chuß╗Śi & 24 flip-flop & 8 flip-flop \\
-\hline
-Sß╗æ chu kß╗│ shift/vector & 24 & 8 \\
-\hline
-Tß╗Ģng shift cycles (64 vectors) & $64 \times 24 = 1536$ & $64 \times 8 = 512$ \\
-\hline
-Sß╗æ ch├ón Scan In & 1 & 3 \\
-\hline
-Sß╗æ ch├ón Scan Out & 1 & 3 \\
-\hline
-Tß╗Ģng ch├ón I/O tß╗æn th├¬m & 2 & 6 \\
-\hline
-Tß╗æc ─æß╗Ö test & Chß║Łm ($\times 1$) & Nhanh gß║źp 3 ($\times 3$) \\
-\hline
-\end{tabular}
-\end{table}
-
-C├┤ng thß╗®c tß╗Ģng qu├Īt khi chia th├Ānh $N$ chuß╗Śi song song bß║▒ng nhau:
-$$T_{multiple} = V \times \frac{L}{N} \times T_{clk}$$
-So vß╗øi Full Scan: $T_{full} = V \times L \times T_{clk}$. Hß╗ć sß╗æ giß║Żm thß╗Øi gian l├Ā $N$ lß║¦n, nhŲ░ng phß║Żi \textbf{─æ├Īnh ─æß╗Ģi bß║▒ng $2 \times N$ ch├ón I/O bß╗Ģ sung} (mß╗Śi chuß╗Śi cß║¦n 1 SI v├Ā 1 SO).
-
-% ======================================================================
-% Mß╗żC 4: C├üC M├ö H├īNH SCAN Cß║żP RTL (Dß╗ŗch tß╗½ gi├Īo tr├¼nh)
-% ======================================================================
-\section{Quy hoß║Īch c├Īc m├┤ h├¼nh Scan cho thiß║┐t kß║┐ cß║źp RTL (7.5.3 Scan Designs for RTL)}
-
-Viß╗ćc lß║Łp kß║┐ hoß║Īch (planning) cho c├Īc thiß║┐t kß║┐ scan kh├Īc nhau ß╗¤ \textbf{mß╗®c RT-level} l├Ā tŲ░ŲĪng ─æß╗æi dß╗ģ d├Āng nß║┐u ta \textbf{nh├¼n nhß║Łn mß║Īch tß╗½ g├│c ─æß╗Ö RTL view} -- tß╗®c l├Ā xem mß║Īch dŲ░ß╗øi dß║Īng c├Īc thanh ghi (registers) v├Ā c├Īc khß╗æi logic tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp kß║┐t nß╗æi ch├║ng. Khi ─æ├Ż lß║Łp kß║┐ hoß║Īch xong, ta c├│ thß╗ā \textbf{Ų░ß╗øc lŲ░ß╗Żng thß╗Øi gian test} (test time estimate). Viß╗ćc triß╗ān khai thß╗▒c tß║┐ (implementation) thiß║┐t kß║┐ scan ─æ├Ż l├¬n kß║┐ hoß║Īch sß║Į ─æŲ░ß╗Żc thß╗▒c hiß╗ćn tr├¬n \textbf{netlist ─æß║¦u ra} cß╗¦a qu├Ī tr├¼nh tß╗Ģng hß╗Żp (synthesis) tß╗½ m├Ż HDL cß║źp RT-level. Viß╗ćc ph├Īt triß╗ān chŲ░ŲĪng tr├¼nh test c┼®ng trß╗¤ n├¬n dß╗ģ d├Āng hŲĪn khi ta suy ngh─® vß╗ü mß║Īch dŲ░ß╗øi dß║Īng \textbf{m├┤ h├¼nh Huffman} sau khi ─æ├Ż ch├©n scan.
-
-\subsection{Partial Scan (Qu├®t Mß╗Öt phß║¦n)}
-Ngo├Āi Full Scan v├Ā Multiple Scan, c├▓n c├│ c├Īc phŲ░ŲĪng ├Īn thiß║┐t kß║┐ scan kh├Īc cho v├Ł dß╗ź RTL cß╗¦a ch├║ng ta, ─æ├│ l├Ā \textbf{Partial Scan} (qu├®t mß╗Öt phß║¦n). Trong Partial Scan, \textbf{kh├┤ng phß║Żi tß║źt cß║Ż} flip-flop ─æß╗üu ─æŲ░ß╗Żc thay bß║▒ng Scan Flip-Flop. Chß╗ē mß╗Öt sß╗æ flip-flop ─æŲ░ß╗Żc chß╗Źn lß╗Źc (thŲ░ß╗Øng l├Ā nhß╗»ng flip-flop nß║▒m tr├¬n c├Īc v├▓ng lß║Ęp phß║Żn hß╗ōi -- feedback loops -- kh├│ kiß╗ām thß╗Ł nhß║źt) mß╗øi ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ch├©n scan.
-
-\textbf{[Bß╗Ģ sung kiß║┐n thß╗®c cß╗¦a nh├│m]} Ų»u ─æiß╗ām cß╗¦a Partial Scan l├Ā giß║Żm ─æ├Īng kß╗ā diß╗ćn t├Łch phß║¦n cß╗®ng bß╗Ģ sung (area overhead) v├Ā giß║Żm ß║Żnh hŲ░ß╗¤ng ─æß║┐n hiß╗ću n─āng (timing/power). Tuy nhi├¬n, nhŲ░ß╗Żc ─æiß╗ām l├Ā \textbf{─æß╗Ö phß╗¦ lß╗Śi (fault coverage)} thŲ░ß╗Øng thß║źp hŲĪn Full Scan, v├Ā c├┤ng cß╗ź ATPG phß║Żi xß╗Ł l├Į m├┤ h├¼nh phß╗®c tß║Īp hŲĪn (v├¼ m├┤ h├¼nh kh├┤ng ho├Ān to├Ān l├Ā tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp -- vß║½n c├▓n mß╗Öt sß╗æ thanh ghi).
-
-\subsection{Multiple Independent Scans (Qu├®t ─Éa luß╗ōng ─Éß╗Öc lß║Łp)}
-Mß╗Öt giß║Żi ph├Īp kh├Īc l├Ā \textbf{Multiple Independent Scans} (qu├®t ─æa luß╗ōng ─æß╗Öc lß║Łp). ├Ø tŲ░ß╗¤ng ß╗¤ ─æ├óy l├Ā nß║┐u ta \textbf{ph├ón chia (partition)} mß║Īch th├Ānh hai phß║¦n ri├¬ng biß╗ćt -- v├Ł dß╗ź phß║¦n \texttt{Datapath} (─æŲ░ß╗Øng dß║½n dß╗» liß╗ću) v├Ā phß║¦n \texttt{Controller} (bß╗Ö ─æiß╗üu khiß╗ān) -- th├¼ c├│ thß╗ā thiß║┐t kß║┐ scan \textbf{ri├¬ng biß╗ćt, ─æß╗Öc lß║Łp} cho tß╗½ng phß║¦n.
-
-\subsection{Isolated Scan (Qu├®t C├┤ lß║Łp)}
-Viß╗ćc t├Īch mß╗Öt mß║Īch th├Ānh nhiß╗üu phß║¦n con (subcomponents) c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc thß╗▒c hiß╗ćn th├┤ng qua kß╗╣ thuß║Łt \textbf{Isolated Scan} (qu├®t c├┤ lß║Łp). Trong kß╗╣ thuß║Łt n├Āy, mß╗Öt thanh ghi dß╗ŗch (shift register) ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ─æß║Ęt tß║Īi \textbf{giao diß╗ćn (interface)} giß╗»a c├Īc phß║¦n con, cho ph├®p dß╗ŗch v├Āo (scan-in) v├Ā dß╗ŗch ra (scan-out) c├Īc gi├Ī trß╗ŗ t├Łn hiß╗ću ß╗¤ ranh giß╗øi giß╗»a ch├║ng. Nhß╗Ø vß║Ły, mß╗Śi phß║¦n con c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc kiß╗ām thß╗Ł \textbf{c├┤ lß║Łp}, ─æß╗Öc lß║Łp vß╗øi c├Īc phß║¦n kh├Īc.
-
-\subsection{C├Īc tham sß╗æ quyß║┐t ─æß╗ŗnh khi lß╗▒a chß╗Źn chiß║┐n lŲ░ß╗Żc Scan}
-Khi lß║Łp kß║┐ hoß║Īch cho c├Īc chiß║┐n lŲ░ß╗Żc scan kh├Īc nhau, c├│ \textbf{3 tham sß╗æ ch├Łnh} cß║¦n c├ón nhß║»c:
-\begin{enumerate}
-    \item \textbf{Sß╗æ lŲ░ß╗Żng test vectors:} C├Āng nhiß╗üu vector th├¼ thß╗Øi gian test c├Āng l├óu, nhŲ░ng ─æß╗Ö phß╗¦ lß╗Śi c├│ thß╗ā cao hŲĪn.
-    \item \textbf{Chiß╗üu d├Āi mß╗Śi test vector:} Phß╗ź thuß╗Öc v├Āo chiß╗üu d├Āi chuß╗Śi scan v├Ā sß╗æ lŲ░ß╗Żng ng├Ą v├Āo/ra song song.
-    \item \textbf{─Éß╗Ö phß╗¦ lß╗Śi (Fault Coverage):} Chß╗ē sß╗æ quan trß╗Źng nhß║źt, thß╗ā hiß╗ćn tß╗Ę lß╗ć phß║¦n tr─ām lß╗Śi c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc ph├Īt hiß╗ćn.
-\end{enumerate}
 
-\textbf{[Bß╗Ģ sung kiß║┐n thß╗®c cß╗¦a nh├│m]} Bß║Żng dŲ░ß╗øi ─æ├óy t├│m tß║»t Ų░u nhŲ░ß╗Żc ─æiß╗ām cß╗¦a tß╗½ng chiß║┐n lŲ░ß╗Żc:
-
-\begin{table}[!ht]
-\centering
-\caption{So s├Īnh c├Īc chiß║┐n lŲ░ß╗Żc Scan Design}
-\label{tab:strategies}
-\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|}
-\hline
-\textbf{Chiß║┐n lŲ░ß╗Żc} & \textbf{Fault Coverage} & \textbf{Area Overhead} & \textbf{Test Time} \\
-\hline
-Full Scan (1 chain) & Cao nhß║źt & Cao nhß║źt & Rß║źt l├óu \\
-\hline
-Multiple Parallel Scan & Cao nhß║źt & Cao (th├¬m ch├ón I/O) & Giß║Żm $N$ lß║¦n \\
-\hline
-Partial Scan & Trung b├¼nh & Thß║źp & Trung b├¼nh \\
-\hline
-Isolated Scan & Tuß╗│ thiß║┐t kß║┐ & Trung b├¼nh & Tuß╗│ ph├ón v├╣ng \\
-\hline
-\end{tabular}
-\end{table}

-% ======================================================================
-% Mß╗żC 5: Tß╗öNG Kß║ŠT CHŲ»ŲĀNG (Dß╗ŗch tß╗½ gi├Īo tr├¼nh)
-% ======================================================================
-\section{Tß╗Ģng kß║┐t chŲ░ŲĪng (7.6 Summary)}
-
-ChŲ░ŲĪng n├Āy ─æ├Ż thß║Żo luß║Łn vß╗ü c├Īc \textbf{kß╗╣ thuß║Łt DFT (Design for Test)}. Ch├║ng ta ─æ├Ż bß║»t ─æß║¦u vß╗øi mß╗Öt sß╗æ \textbf{phŲ░ŲĪng ph├Īp ─æß║Ęc th├╣ (ad hoc methods)} v├Ā tß╗½ ─æ├│ ph├Īt triß╗ān dß║¦n l├¬n tß╗øi \textbf{Isolated Scan}. Tß║źt cß║Ż c├Īc phŲ░ŲĪng ph├Īp n├Āy ─æß╗üu c├│ thß╗ā ├Īp dß╗źng cho cß║Ż mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp (combinational) lß║½n mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ (sequential).
-
-Kß╗╣ thuß║Łt \textbf{Scan Design} m├Ā ch├║ng ta bß║»t ─æß║¦u thß║Żo luß║Łn tß╗½ Mß╗źc 7.3 chß╗¦ yß║┐u tß║Łp trung v├Āo \textbf{mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒}, v├Ā mß╗źc ─æ├Łch ch├Łnh cß╗¦a n├│ l├Ā \textbf{biß║┐n ─æß╗Ģi mß║Īch tuß║¦n tß╗▒ th├Ānh mß║Īch tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp} (tß╗½ g├│c nh├¼n kiß╗ām thß╗Ł), sao cho c├Īc phŲ░ŲĪng ph├Īp sinh test tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp (combinational TG methods) c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc sß╗Ł dß╗źng ─æß╗ā tß║Īo mß║½u kiß╗ām thß╗Ł.
-
-C├Īc thiß║┐t kß║┐ scan kh├Īc m├Ā ch├║ng ta ─æ├Ż thß║Żo luß║Łn vß╗ü cŲĪ bß║Żn ─æß╗üu tu├ón theo \textbf{c├╣ng mß╗Öt nguy├¬n tß║»c}. Trong \textbf{Partial Scan methods}, viß╗ćc ─æŲ░a v├Āo c├Īc ng├Ą v├Āo giß║Ż (pseudo inputs) v├Ā ng├Ą ra giß║Ż (pseudo outputs) \textbf{kh├┤ng ho├Ān to├Ān} biß║┐n ─æß╗Ģi m├┤ h├¼nh tuß║¦n tß╗▒ th├Ānh m├┤ h├¼nh tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp -- c├Īc thanh ghi vß║½n c├▓n tß╗ōn tß║Īi trong m├┤ h├¼nh thu ─æŲ░ß╗Żc. Mß║Ęc d├╣ vß║Ły, m├┤ h├¼nh thu ─æŲ░ß╗Żc \textbf{vß║½n c├│ thß╗ā} ─æŲ░ß╗Żc sß╗Ł dß╗źng cho qu├Ī tr├¼nh sinh test tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp. Sß╗▒ sai lß╗ćch (deviation) so vß╗øi m├┤ h├¼nh tß╗Ģ hß╗Żp thuß║¦n tu├Į sß║Į ß║Żnh hŲ░ß╗¤ng ─æß║┐n \textbf{thß╗¦ tß╗źc test} (test procedure), trong ─æ├│ cß║¦n bß╗Ģ sung th├¬m c├Īc \textbf{xung nhß╗ŗp phß╗ź (extra clock pulses)} ─æß╗ā b├╣ ─æß║»p cho nhß╗»ng thanh ghi vß║½n c├▓n lß║Īi trong m├┤ h├¼nh.
-
-─Éß╗ā minh hoß║Ī viß╗ćc ß╗®ng dß╗źng phŲ░ŲĪng ph├Īp scan cho \textbf{thiß║┐t kß║┐ cß║źp RT-level} v├Ā chß╗®ng minh t├Łnh khß║Ż thi cß╗¦a c├Īc kß╗╣ thuß║Łt n├Āy, mß╗źc cuß╗æi c├╣ng ─æ├Ż sß╗Ł dß╗źng mß╗Öt thiß║┐t kß║┐ RT-level nhß╗Å gß╗Źn kß║┐t hß╗Żp vß╗øi \textbf{Verilog testbench} ─æß╗ā kiß╗ām thß╗Ł scan ─æ├Ż ch├©n. C├Īc thiß║┐t kß║┐ lß╗øn hŲĪn c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc \textbf{ph├ón chia (partition)} th├Ānh nhiß╗üu phß║¦n con (subcomponents), trong ─æ├│ mß╗Śi phß║¦n con c├│ thß╗ā y├¬u cß║¦u mß╗Öt \textbf{dß║Īng scan kh├Īc nhau}. Viß╗ćc t├Īch biß╗ćt thiß║┐t kß║┐ th├Ānh c├Īc phß║¦n con c├│ thß╗ā ─æŲ░ß╗Żc thß╗▒c hiß╗ćn bß║▒ng \textbf{thanh ghi dß╗ŗch (shift register)} ─æß╗ā scan-in v├Ā scan-out c├Īc gi├Ī trß╗ŗ t├Łn hiß╗ću tß║Īi giao diß╗ćn (interface) giß╗»a c├Īc phß║¦n con.
-
-% ======================================================================

+import PyPDF2
+import os
+
+pdf_path = "d:/Phd/EE5217_Tue/refs/3-Digital System Test and Testable Design_ Using HDL Models and Architectures.pdf"
+reader = PyPDF2.PdfReader(pdf_path)
+text = ""
+start_page = 200
+end_page = min(301, len(reader.pages))
+for i in range(start_page, end_page):
+    text += reader.pages[i].extract_text() + "\n"
+with open("d:/Phd/EE5217_Tue/Chapter7_Pages200_300.txt", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+    f.write(text)
+print(f"Extracted pages {start_page} to {end_page} successfully to Chapter7_Pages200_300.txt")

+Preface
+
+This is a book on test and testability of digital circuits in which test is spoken in the language of
+design. In this book, the concepts of testing and testability are treated together with digital design
+practices and methodologies. We show how testing digital circuits designing testable circuits can
+take advantage of some of the well-established RT-level design and verification methodologies and
+tools. The book uses Verilog models and testbenches for implementing and explaining fault simula-
+tion and test generation algorithms. In the testability part, it describes various scan and BIST meth-
+ods in Verilog and uses Verilog testbenches as virtual testers to examine and evaluate these
+testability methods. In designing testable circuits, we use Verilog testbenches to evaluate, and thus
+improve testability of a design.
+
+   The first part of the book develops Verilog test environments that can perform gate-level fault
+simulation and test generation. This part uses Verilog PLI along with VerilogŌĆÖs powerful testbench
+development facilities for modeling hardware and programing test environments. The second part
+of the book uses Verilog as a hardware design tool for describing DFT and BIST hardware. In this
+part, Verilog is used as a hardware description language describing synthesizable testable hardware.
+Throughout the book, Verilog simulation helps developing and evaluating test methods and test-
+ability hardware constructs.
+
+   This book professes a new approach to teaching test. Use of Verilog and Verilog PLI for test
+applications is what distinguishes this book from other test and testability books. As HDLs were
+used in late 1970s for teaching computer architectures, today, HDLs can be used to illustrate test
+methodologies and testability architectures that are otherwise illustrated informally by flow charts,
+graphs, and block diagrams. Verilog eliminates ambiguities in test algorithms and BIST and DFT
+hardware architectures, and it clearly describes the architecture of the testability hardware and its
+test sessions. Describing on-chip test hardware in Verilog helps evaluating the related algorithms in
+terms of hardware overhead and timing and thus feasibility of using them on SoC chips. Further
+support for this approach comes in use of testbenches. Using PLI in developing testbenches and
+virtual testers gives us a powerful programing tool interfaced with hardware described in Verilog.
+This mixed hardware/software environment facilitates the description of complex test programs and
+test strategies.
+
+                                                                                                                                          vii
+wwwwwwwwwwww
+Acknowledgments
+
+When I first thought of using a hardware description language for test purposes, I started using
+VHDL models for test purposes in my course on digital system testing at the University of Tehran.
+After several years of teaching this course, we switched to Verilog and a set of library components
+that facilitated this usage of Verilog was developed. The groups of students who developed the
+software and helped me in the formation of the materials are important contributors to this work.
+The student, who took the responsibility for the development of the software package was Nastaran
+Nemati. She managed the development of the complete library by the time of her graduation in
+2010. Her efforts contributed significantly to this work. I thank my students at Worcester Polytechnic
+Institute in Massachusetts, USA, and the University of Tehran for sitting at my presentations or
+watching them online and making useful suggestions.
+
+   When the actual development of the book started, my graduate student, Fatemeh (Negin)
+Javaheri, became the key person with whom I discussed my ideas. She was always available for
+consulting with me and her ideas helped significantly in shaping the structure of the book. She later
+took responsibility for developing the material for the chapter on test compression. Negin continues
+to work with me on my research, and she is looking forward to the next book that I want to write.
+Another important contributor, also a graduate student at the University of Tehran, is Somayeh
+Sadeghi Kohan. Somayeh developed the materials for the chapter on boundary scan, and in the final
+stages of this work she was very helpful reviewing chapters and suggesting changes. The feedbacks
+she provided and changes she suggested were most helpful. Nastaran Nemati helped developing the
+HDL chapter, and Parisa Kabiri and Atie Lotfi also contributed to some of the chapters and helped
+reviewing the materials.
+
+   As always, and as it is with all my books, Fatemeh Asgari, who has been my assistant for the
+past 20 years, became responsible for managing the project. She managed the group of students who
+did research, developed software, collected materials, and prepared the final manuscript with its text
+and artwork. FatemehŌĆÖs support and management of my writing and research projects have always
+been key to the successful completion of these projects. I cannot thank her enough for the work she
+has done for me throughout the years.
+
+   My work habits and time I spend away from my family working on my research and writing
+projects have been particularly difficult for them. However, I have always had their support, under-
+standing, and encouragement for all my projects. My wife, Irma, has always been a great help for
+me providing an environment that I can spend hours and hours on my writing projects. I thank Irma,
+and my sons Aarash and Arvand.
+

+Introduction
+
+The main focus of the book is on digital systems test and design testability. The book uses Verilog
+for design, test analysis, and testability of digital systems. In the first chapter, we discuss the basics
+of test and testable design while discussing the aspects of test that hardware description languages
+can be used for. This part discusses the entire digital system testing in terms of test methods, test-
+ability methods, and testing methods, and it discusses the use of HDLs in each aspect. After the
+introductory parts, we have included a chapter on the basics of Verilog and using this language for
+design and test. The body of the book assumes this basic Verilog-based RT-level design knowledge
+and builds test and simulation concepts upon that.
+
+   Starting in Chap. 3, the focus of the book turns to test issues, such as fault collapsing, fault simu-
+lation, and test generation. This part that is regarded as covering ŌĆ£test methodsŌĆØ has four chapters
+that start with the presentation of fault models, followed by fault simulation methods, and then two
+chapters on test generation, discussing random HDL-based test generation and deterministic test.
+For such applications, we use Verilog gate models and PLI-based testbenches that are capable of
+injecting faults and performing fault simulation and test generation. In this part, Verilog testbenches
+act as test programs for managing the structural model of a circuit for performing fault simulation,
+calculating fault coverage, and test pattern generation.
+
+   The testability part of the book begins in Chap. 7. There are four chapters in this part (Chaps.
+7ŌĆō10) in which various testability methods, built-in self-test architectures, and test compression
+methods are discussed. We use Verilog coding for describing the hardware of various testability
+methods and BIST architectures. Verilog testbenches, in this part, act as virtual testers examining
+testability and test hardware embedded in a design. Together, PLI-based fault simulation and test
+generation Verilog environments of Chaps. 3ŌĆō6, and Verilog coding of testability hardware provide
+a complete environment for testability and BIST evaluation. Design and refinement of test hardware
+can be achieved after such evaluations. This part also discusses IEEE standards for boundary scan
+and core testing, and uses Verilog for describing these standards and using them in designs. The last
+chapter is on memory testing with a focus on MBIST that we describe in Verilog.
+
+Chapters
+
+Basics of Test and Role of HDLs
+
+Basics of test are covered in this chapter. We talk about the importance of digital system testing and
+define various test terminologies. Economy of test is discussed and reducing test time by means of
+better test methods, more testable designs, and more efficient testing is discussed. Relation between
+design and test are discussed in this chapter.
+
+                                                                                                                                         xix
+xx                               Introduction
+
+Verilog HDL for Design and Test
+
+This chapter talks about the Verilog hardware description language for the description of digital
+systems and the corresponding testbenches. We discuss combinational and sequential circuit model-
+ing and present several examples. Only the key language constructs that is needed for understanding
+models and architectures in the rest of the book are presented here. This chapter shows the use of
+Verilog for developing good design testbenches. Several templates for testbench development are
+discussed. In doing so, the use of PLI and developing PLI functions are presented. The testbench
+part is extended in the chapters that follow.
+
+Fault and Defect Modeling
+
+Transistor and gate-level faults are described first. Verilog simulations show the correspondence
+between lower-level transistor faults and upper level gate faults. We discuss functional and struc-
+tural faults and the distinction between them. Structural gate-level faults are discussed and the jus-
+tification of this model is illustrated by the use of simulations. We elaborate on the stuck-at fault
+model and show PLI functions for fault injection. After this, the chapter discusses fault equivalence
+and several fault collapsing techniques. We develop Verilog and PLI functions and testbenches for
+generating fault lists and fault collapsing. Several benchmark circuits are tested with these
+testbenches.
+
+Fault Simulation Applications and Methods
+
+The chapter begins discussing the use of fault simulation and its applications in design and test. We
+then discuss various fault simulation techniques, including serial, parallel, concurrent, deductive,
+differential, and critical path tracing fault simulation. For several of these methods, we develop a
+testbench that injects stuck-at faults and performs simulations. Verilog PLI-based testbenches for
+partial implementation of other fault simulation techniques are also discussed. Fault dictionaries are
+discussed and created using Verilog and PLI testbenches. Using these utilities, we also discuss and
+implement test coverage, fault dropping, and other fault simulation-related concepts. The format of
+fault lists is taken from the previous chapter in which Verilog and PLI testbenches generated such
+lists. Several complete Verilog testbenches with PLI are developed and utilized in this chapter.
+
+Test Pattern Generation Methods
+
+This chapter begins with the presentation of various testability techniques, including probability
+based, structural, and SCOPE parameter calculation. PLI functions in Verilog testbenches are devel-
+oped for calculating controllability and observability parameters of internal nodes of gate-level
+circuits. Detectability and its role in the determination of random tests are also discussed. After this
+first part, we discuss various random test generation methods and take advantage of testability
+m┬Ł easures of the first part. This chapter uses Verilog testbenches for generating random tests and
+evaluating them by Verilog-based fault simulation.
+Introduction                   xxi
+
+Deterministic Test Generation
+
+We started the presentation of test generation in Chap. 5 with presenting random test generation. This
+chapter discusses deterministic test generation, which we consider Phase 2 of the test generation
+process. We discuss algorithms like, the D-algorithm, PODEM, CPT, and some of the simplified and
+derivatives of these algorithms. Verilog testbenches using Verilog PLI functions for deciding when
+to stop random test generation and when to start deterministic TG are developed. Test compaction
+can be regarded as the next phase of test generation. A part of this chapter is dedicated to this topic
+and several test compaction methods and their Verilog implementations are discussed.
+
+Design for Test by Means of Scan
+
+In this and the chapters that follow, in addition to using Verilog for developing test environments,
+Verilog is also used for describing actual hardware constructs. In this chapter on DFT, we show
+synthesizable Verilog codes for the DFT architectures that we present. The chapter begins with the
+presentation of several ad hoc design-for-test techniques. We then show full-scan and various par-
+tial-scan architectures, and for the purpose of unambiguous description of such hardware structures,
+their corresponding Verilog codes are shown. Test methods and testbenches that we developed in
+the previous chapters of this book are utilized here for scan design evaluations and refinements. We
+show how a testbench can be used for helping us configure a scan design and generate tests for it.
+We also show how a Verilog PLI testbench can be used for the application and testing of a scan-
+based design. This latter application of testbenches is what we refer to as a virtual tester.
+
+Standard IEEE Test Access Methods
+
+This chapter discusses IEEE 1149.1 test standards. Hardware structures corresponding to these
+standards are discussed in Verilog. Virtual testers that operate board and core testing hardware are
+described as Verilog testbenches. Through the use of Verilog, we are able to show the architecture
+and utilization of these standards. Interfacing between various components of IEEE Std.1149.1 and
+how the standard interacts with the circuit under test, on one side, and the test equipment, on the
+other side, are clarified here by the use of Verilog hardware descriptions and testbenches.
+
+Logic Built-in Self-test
+
+This chapter starts with the methods of designing on-chip test data generation and output analysis.
+We then incorporate these components in built-in self-test architectures for on-chip testing. In this
+chapter, we show synthesizable Verilog codes for all BIST architectures that we present. We show
+classical BIST architectures, such as RTS, BILBO, and BEST; furthermore, on-line BIST, concur-
+rent BIST, and BISTs for special architectures are discussed. We use Verilog for unambiguous
+description of such hardware structures. And, we use Verilog and PLI testbenches, which we devel-
+oped for fault injection and fault simulation, for BIST evaluation and configuration. Determination
+of BIST test sessions for a better fault coverage and the determination of the corresponding signa-
+tures are done by the use of Verilog simulations.
+xxii                                                  Introduction
+
+Test Data Compression
+
+This chapter discusses test compression techniques, their usage in design-for-test, and their corre-
+sponding hardware implementations. We discuss Huffman, Run-length, Golomb, and other coding
+techniques used in test compression. In addition, scan compression techniques and their correspond-
+ing on-chip scan structures are discussed. Compression algorithms are discussed and hardware for
+decompression hardware as it is placed on a chip is described in Verilog.
+
+Memory Testing by Means of Memory BIST
+
+This chapter begins with a presentation of memory structures and corresponding fault models.
+Various March test techniques are described and an analysis justifying various test algorithms is
+given. We then discuss several memory BIST architectures and show their corresponding Verilog
+descriptions. Operating an MBIST is demonstrated by means of a Verilog testbench.
+
+Appendixes
+

+B. Gate Components for PLI Test Applications
+C. PLI Test Utilities
+D. IEEE Std.1149.1 Boundary Scan Verilog Description
+E. Boundary Scan IEEE Std.1149.1 Virtual Tester
+F. Generating Netlist by RTL Synthesis (NetlistGen)
+Software and Course Materials
+
+The material for this book has been developed while teaching courses on test and testability at sev-
+eral universities. We are making these materials available.
+
+   We have developed a set of Verilog PLI functions for test development that have been used in
+several courses, and have reached an acceptable maturity. For test applications using original
+RT-level designs, a software program for a netlist generation has been developed that uses Xilinx
+ISE to synthesize and convert the output to a netlist that our PLI functions can use. Many of the
+netlists of the examples used in this book have been generated by this software. Applications that
+can be performed with the set of our PLI functions and netlist include fault collapsing, random test
+generation, fault simulation, and testability measurements. Also, virtual testers discussed in this
+book use the netlist and PLI functions for simulation.
+
+   Presentation materials for all the chapters are also available (PowerPoint slides). In addition,
+when teaching this course, I have noticed that students sometimes need a review of Verilog or logic
+design concepts. I have developed short videos that students can use to get ready for the material
+presented in the book. Videos and manuals for the use of software are also available and can be
+obtained from the author.
+

+materials and software programs are being developed. Interested readers and users can contact the
